Settled component-roster note (persona-listen/persona-speak audio components); implementation detail now in INTENT.md/code.
Time-bound roadmap-priority statement (multi-machine criome elevated to near-term); transient, not a durable protocol-design arrow.

survivors.desc: Cite a Spirit intent record in prose by quoting its description summary literally as bracketed text plus a (Spirit Kind short) parenthetical; the bracketed substance is the citation, not the opaque number. Quote central intents literally in a prominent place, especially in psyche-facing reports. Applies to all agents.
survivors.reason: hgvg (cite by bracket-quoted description) and xyqt (cite as bracket-quote plus Kind/short parenthetical) are the same citation arrow; merged.

survivors.desc: Psyche reports speak to a human in a narrative voice: every chat response paraphrases an accompanying per-response report, carrying 3-7 top items spread across (a) intent questions/clarifications, (b) how-new-mechanisms-work observations, (c) recent-work examples. Use Spirit IDs to mark ranges of intent, not as per-claim inline citations.
survivors.reason: ri4n (human-narrative report voice, ranges over per-claim citations) and k24p (chat paraphrases a report, 3-7 items across three categories) both define psyche-facing report/chat shape; merged.
Distinct still-relevant rule: chat prints full relative report paths, not short locators.

survivors.desc: Build event surfaces, not polling: producers push events and consumers subscribe; when a mechanism only offers polling, escalate into the stack to add a real event surface rather than tuning intervals.
survivors.reason: Distinct load-bearing architecture arrow; de-negated to affirmative.

survivors.desc: A Spirit short identifier is the shortest-unique base36-lowercase prefix of the record's random hash, minimum three characters, scoped per kind, extended only on same-kind collision. The full random hash stays binary on the wire; only the short prefix appears in text.
survivors.reason: 6kfz (short-identifier derivation) and kjwg (full hash binary, short in text) describe the same identifier scheme; merged.

survivors.desc: Report filename convention is reports/<role>/<N>-<Variant>-<topic>-<date>.md: a capitalized Variant naming the report kind (Psyche/Design/Audit/Research/Synthesis/Closeout/Handover) plus one or more hyphen-separated topic labels between number and title, reports staying in their lane subdirectory. Topics come from a per-workspace controlled vocabulary and enable filename topic-grep without opening files. Documented in skills/reporting.md.
survivors.reason: amb5 (topic between number and title), jwmf (Variant + topic + lane convention), 2dhc (documented in reporting.md), eohi (enables topic-grep), lw51 (controlled topic vocabulary) all specify the report filename/topic convention; merged.
Distinct durable rule: Spirit guardian admits affirmatively-stated captures, returns negation-defined ones for rewording.
Distinct durable arrow: re-ground the psyche top-down when they lose the thread, as a precondition for proceeding.
Distinct technical arrow: schema file root identity from filename context; root name not in file body.

survivors.desc: Canonical prose spells criome for the authentication component and criomos for the operating system; on-disk path/code-identifier spellings are preserved when cited. The psyche-facing approval component is mentci (repos mentci-egui, mentci-lib); the earlier Menchie spelling and the Menchie referent were a speech-to-text error to migrate to mentci.
survivors.reason: cx2m (criome/criomos canonical spelling) and 1rqy (mentci not Menchie) are both naming-canon corrections for the criome family; merged.

removed.reason: Settled report kind/topic dimension proposal now absorbed into the report filename convention survivor (jwmf/amb5) and skills/reporting.md; the design narration is no longer load-bearing.
YAML front-matter report header decision is an implementation detail now living in skills/reporting.md.
Correction of a past transient report-header drift/hallucination; the affirmative rule lives in reporting.md (italicised one-liner).
BookOfGoldragon public writing surface plus one-off plasma-practice authorization; settled private/public-surface detail, no longer a durable system arrow.
human-interaction skill must-read-by-every-harness is now encoded in AGENTS.md required reading; settled.
Editor role/lane now exists and is documented in AGENTS.md roles; settled fact, no longer durable intent.
Report agglomeration-by-topic was a transient migration order, now effectively complete; not a durable arrow.
Intent logging uses Spirit is a settled fact stated everywhere (AGENTS.md); redundant stub.
Correction of a past transient agent error (hedging about where an explanation lived); the durable rule lives in AGENTS.md reports-go-in-files discipline.
Agents clarify psyche wording into the Spirit description is a one-line stub fully covered by intent-log/spirit-cli skills and the Spirit-gate override.

survivors.desc: A component contract (signal-<component>, meta-signal-<component>) carries only wire messaging vocabulary: Signal Input/Output roots, their record types, and the wire codec. Nexus and SEMA stay daemon-internal; SignalEngine/NexusEngine/SemaEngine live in the daemon, which imports the contract Input/Output. Clients send and receive only Signal messages.
survivors.reason: Load-bearing contract-vs-daemon split; stripped the cloud-port-error narration and audit aside, kept the rule.

survivors.desc: Spirit write acknowledgements are token-cheap: creation returns only the created record UID (not a SemaReceipt with UID plus database marker), and acks never echo submitted intent content.
survivors.reason: Both express the same arrow: minimal Spirit write replies.

survivors.desc: The agent component models LLM/cloud providers as a generic OpenAI-compatible API (endpoint, model, key as config), so adding a provider (e.g. DeepSeek) is configuration not a contract change; deployment surfaces supply config and secrets. Support may be build-time opt-in; capability observation distinguishes built-but-unconfigured from not-built, and unsupported requests return a typed unsupported-capability reply.
survivors.reason: Four records circling one arrow: how the agent component handles providers (config-driven, build-selectable, typed capability reply).

survivors.desc: The persona-pi extension adapts Pi APIs into the workspace signal-data-tree philosophy rather than mirroring Pi's flat namespace: methods belong to nouns, typed records over flat functions, recursive enum-shaped namespaces (a flat name like query_x_from_behind becomes an object with from_front/from_behind methods). Underlying Pi machinery stays Pi-shaped.
survivors.reason: q7u5 is the terse restatement of yr2w; merge keeping the concrete translation example.

survivors.desc: Lane management moves from the transitional bash tool tools/orchestrate to persona-orchestrate (the typed component), with the lane registry and claim/lock surface in scope; lane registration and lock acquisition become first-class operations on its working signal. The discipline (role) field is an open string-shaped identifier, so new roles are added via one owner-channel operation without contract bumps.
survivors.reason: Both about the persona-orchestrate lane registry contract; one gives destination, one gives the open-role typing.

survivors.desc: Each typed symbol (type, variant, field, operation, route) has a fully-qualified SymbolPath identity through the interface's global namespace — the workspace's canonical machine-readable universal symbol form, not a per-design convention. Machines reason about identity via the qualified path; NOTA renders the same path as human-readable text at user-facing edges.
survivors.reason: Distinct durable arrow: SymbolPath as universal symbol form.

survivors.desc: Every PascalCase name in the schema header (Input/Output root enum bodies, NexusWork, NexusAction, SemaWriteInput, SemaReadInput) becomes an exported top-level type importable by library consumers. The header IS the component's public type-level interface: each variant is a named, defined, exported thing — the contract of verbs and replies the component supports.
survivors.reason: Distinct arrow: schema header as the exported public interface.

survivors.desc: An interface is a root enum with more than one variant; a root naming only one operation is an incomplete design and a newtype, not an interface. Input and Output are the two primary interface roots; payloads are structs (leaf data) or enums (nested interfaces), and interface chain depth measures design realism.
survivors.reason: Distinct definitional arrow: what makes something an interface.

survivors.desc: Each engine trait's method count matches the number of distinct wire events that plane handles: SignalEngine has two (triage on input arrival, reply on output emission), NexusEngine has one (execute, a synchronous compute step), SemaEngine has two (apply for writes, observe for parallel reads). The spec follows method-count-matches-wire-events.
survivors.reason: Distinct rule binding engine trait shape to wire events; dropped the designer/spirit divergence narration.

survivors.desc: When a match between two domains has no defined behavior in a cell, the response is a typed error or help message (Unavailable / Unauthorized / NotImplemented / etc.) that lives in the domain enum as part of the trait surface, so the caller can match on it — not an exception or unhandled default.
survivors.reason: Distinct arrow: undefined-cell responses are typed and in the enum.
Distinct still-relevant arrow: config changes flow through the meta-signal socket.

survivors.desc: The schema daemon resolves and caches schemas in memory and owns the namespace surface; agents and code emitters query it to resolve namespace references, so all cross-schema resolution flows through it. It is the runtime arm of the precompiled-schemas library.
survivors.reason: Distinct architectural arrow: schema daemon owns namespace resolution.

removed.reason: General no-parallel-API hygiene now covered by the workspace 'no backward compatibility pre-production' override and one-active-API discipline in AGENTS.md; adds no unique still-relevant arrow.

Low-certainty gap-fill framing the wire protocol as REST; speculative architectural narration not a settled load-bearing decision, superseded by the concrete Signal contract / SymbolPath model now in place.

survivors.desc: Every component has exactly two contracts: signal-<component> (working signal) and meta-signal-<component> (meta policy/authority signal) — no third, no owner-signal split. Each exposes two thin CLI clients (<component> and meta-<component>) as one extracted pattern. MetaSignal is canonical (OwnerSignal deprecated); from-scratch policy contracts are born meta-signal. Every daemon binds and serves its meta socket at runtime even when its only op is Configure; meta-signal is optional only for ownerless components, while a component with an owner (e.g. message) gains the meta tier so owner identity arrives as authenticated policy without redeploy. signal-persona is canonical (not a shim); meta-signal-persona is to be created; signal-engine-management folds into the persona pair.
survivors.reason: All express the universal two-contracts-plus-two-CLIs component shape, MetaSignal naming, the always-bound meta socket, and the owner-policy plane.

survivors.desc: Each component engine defines its Signal, Nexus, and SEMA interfaces in schema and runs core logic through schema-emitted traits whose methods take/return root types. Separation is strict: SEMA owns all durable state/storage, Nexus owns all decision-making and request-execution (Nexus is the execution engine, superseding executor), Signal owns all communication; no concern lives outside its engine. Flow: Signal in -> Nexus -> SEMA (when state work) -> Nexus -> Signal -> client. Handwritten runtime stays terse over schema-emitted nouns; daemon main reduces to a small generated call; domain logic lives in non-default trait-impl algorithms. Every engine has an object owning its logic; reusable engine-role names are traits, not one component's concrete enum. This runtime LOGIC triad (Signal + internal-decision + SEMA) is distinct from the repo PACKAGING triad (component + signal-* + meta-signal-*).
survivors.reason: All state the strict triad engine separation, schema-emitted trait surface, the request flow, and the logic-vs-packaging distinction.

survivors.desc: The Nexus interface — verbs and objects in the nexus schema — is the engine's internal feature interface, existing for VISIBILITY: every computation, filter/condition, conditional write, or internal logic-feature is a declared Nexus verb+object so the full feature surface is a readable catalog, never inline hidden logic. Nexus covers any layer running typed-in -> typed-out: internal IO, external calls (e.g. cloud invoking Cloudflare CLI), and all UIs, unifying them under one execution plane. Nexus input is facts/replies/events to decide from; output is commands/emissions — the two sides are asymmetric, not symmetric lists. Nexus may be a recursive computation destination: next-work is a Nexus object rather than a final reply, carrying objects from Signal, SEMA, or other schemas.
survivors.reason: All define what Nexus is: the internal-feature catalog, its scope, its input/output asymmetry, and its recursive-continuation model.

survivors.desc: Center design on schema types: components' data types are schema-defined and reasoned about through their schemas, and schema-emitted types thread end to end in logic rather than being re-derived by hand. Prefer value enums that name a field's closed set of cases over stringly-typed fields. A struct field's role is its type, so no two fields share a type (dimensional correctness: Height vs Width); field-name defaults to type-name and an explicit dot-prefixed name signals a missing type or a collection (repetition is a keyed collection, never repeated fields). Schema struct declarations are positional lists of types mirroring NOTA reading order; the name-value struct form and star shorthand are retired. TypeReference application uses the flat head form (Map K V).
survivors.reason: All are schema-type-design principles: schema-centered types, value-enums-over-strings, field-role-is-type, positional declarations, flat TypeReference form.

survivors.desc: Intent logging is the most important part of the system and dwarfs everything else; recorded intent is precious, tightly-controlled data, the center the rest of the design serves. The Spirit guardian is the single locus of judgment: the model checks everything semantic — consistency, duplication, trample, non-intent — with only structural admission staying upstream as input validation.
survivors.reason: Both center the intent layer; the guardian record specifies how that primacy is enforced.

survivors.desc: Schema files live with their contract's source-of-truth; a repo may hold multiple .schema files (one per contract), named <crate>/<contract-name>.schema. Wire-contract/signal schemas live in signal-<component> because the wire shape is the cross-component compilation boundary Rust must see as one canonical source; stable public signal schemas sit in contract repos separate from daemon logic so consumers aren't rebuilt on internal changes. Storage-contract and internal actor-message schemas live with the daemon because daemon+database and internal actors are one process/compilation unit. A daemon's configuration type lives in the signal contract (imported for binary startup decode; the meta-signal Configure op wraps the same type), not hand-written in the daemon. Signal repos carry only signal/wire vocabulary; runtime-plane, storage, provider-effect, and REST schema belong in the runtime component.
survivors.reason: All decide where each kind of schema lives: contract source-of-truth, wire in signal-*, storage/actor/config with daemon, config sourced from signal contract.

survivors.desc: A contract is a channel and each channel gets its own schema: the repo (wire) contract, the storage contract, and each internal-actor-message vocabulary are channels, so one component can have multiple schemas (one per channel-contract) — superseding one-schema-per-component. A component is at least three separate plane schema files: the Signal wire contract lives wire-only in the contract repo; Nexus and SEMA plane schemas are separate .schema files INSIDE the daemon crate (e.g. cloud/schema/nexus.schema), each with its own imports/exports/namespace and importing the wire Signal IO. Plane schemas are not separate crates or repos. Schemas warrant from need-to-interact: every part worth describing gets a schema so the psyche can inspect and interact with it, and signal-frame itself is schema. These schema-crystallizes-architecture principles are load-bearing and embedded across ESSENCE/AGENTS/skills/INTENT/ARCHITECTURE.
survivors.reason: All express the contract-is-a-channel, schema-per-channel, plane-schemas-inside-daemon model and its load-bearing status.

survivors.desc: CLIs are thin Signal clients.
survivors.reason: Distinct minimal arrow: a CLI is a thin Signal client, the daemon's first client.
Unique arrow: the final self-improving engine endpoint.

survivors.desc: A programming language is in essence a set of structural macros — constructors taking a typed, counted set of objects (Rust's struct/enum/fn/impl/generics included). Conventional languages freeze that macro set in the compiler; this substrate treats it as data, so the language is infinitely extensible (constructs added as data, not compiler changes) and far easier for an LLM to read/write/reason about. Everything is data conforming to a schema type — a macro is a value of a struct whose pattern and template are ordinary typed fields; no privileged escape-hatch. A file's structure is a typed tree of structural-form nodes that recognise a block by shape and recursively decode children: the grammar is the type, the type is the reader, so no parser is hand-written (the aski elegant-syntax goal falls out of declaring typed nodes as data). The structural-macro-node grammar and NOTA's own grammar are schema-expressible, making the notation self-hosting; NOTA's delimiter set is a closed enum (three base pairs + three piped variants, two reserved as extension points). The next step is building that schema spec for the macro node.
survivors.reason: All express the language-as-data / macros-as-data / self-hosting-grammar thesis and the no-hand-written-parser realization.
Unique: prose-in-separate-files via include_str.

survivors.desc: A dedicated program-agnostic session-control component, named terminal-control (contracts signal-terminal-control and meta-signal-terminal-control), owns the terminal session surface: durable name-to-socket session registry, signal control-plane relay, per-session prompt-pattern registry, and session-observation Sema. It is folded into neither harness (AI-only, would orphan non-harness cells) nor orchestrate (which owns coordination not delivery identity); orchestrate owns cell-instance lifecycle policy as an order plane. terminal-cell stays the abduco primitive and absorbs the input-gate writer and prompt-matcher currently duplicated with terminal.
survivors.reason: All concern the terminal-control component: its shape, boundaries, and the terminal-cell primitive.

survivors.desc: Components with two authority surfaces use the two-contract triad shape: two schemas (signal-<c> working/peer-callable and meta-signal-<c> policy/owner-only), each emitting its own Signal/Nexus/SEMA engines and sharing record types, with the runtime crate importing both and running two listeners on two sockets. lojix ships two CLIs accordingly: a meta-socket CLI speaking meta-signal-lojix (Deploy/Pin/Unpin/Retire, daemon config, owner SO_PEERCRED-authenticated) and a regular-socket CLI speaking signal-lojix peer ops (Query/WatchDeployments/WatchCacheRetention/Unwatch). The schema-derived cloud port uses signal-cloud (read-only Observe/Validate) and meta-signal-cloud (eight account/plan mutations). cloud and domain components follow the current triad signal/sema/executor architecture; future component work uses this latest bottom architecture rather than older ad hoc shapes.
survivors.reason: All instantiate the two-socket/two-contract/two-CLI triad shape (lojix, cloud) and the directive to use the latest triad architecture.

survivors.desc: The schema-derived engine mechanism (designer 482 / psyche report 1) is workspace-canonical: NexusWork/NexusAction asymmetric pair + the five-variant action set (ReplyToSignal, CommandSemaWrite, CommandSemaRead, CommandEffect, Continue); macro-generated runner loop (triad_main! from schema-rust-next); per-component effects declared in schema (Stash first); Continue as in-process recursion; cross-component invocation via Signal contracts not Nexus-internal access. These reaction-frame types (Work, Action, the five-variant set) are workspace-universal — declared once and bound per component, never re-authored. The emitted triad engines ARE kameo actors (actors everywhere; synchronous mutex-wrapped daemon emission is drift); a minimal kameo actor with on_start/on_stop adopts kameo now while deeper runtime machinery (backpressure, scheduling) stays deferred. Runtime nouns named Actor must be real Kameo actors. A universal Interact trait — interact(other) defining how two objects interact, async when awaiting outbound queries like criome authorization — formalizes that all logic is interactions between domains.
survivors.reason: All ratify/refine the designer-482 engine mechanism: action set, universal reaction frames, actors-everywhere, Interact trait.
Unique load-bearing why: deterministic answers are mechanism, agents reserved for non-derivable cognition.
Schema files are strict typed interface contracts forcing schema-emitted communication.

survivors.desc: The schema-codegen capability set is closed and covers the engine's structural needs (types, generic frame expansion, payload structs/newtypes/enums, standard impls, role/marker traits, shape-computed constants, method-bearing trait surface, actor wiring, plane carriers, opt-in Deref). The next phase is integration and migration, not language design: flip scalar-newtype impls default-on, integrate onto code-repo main, and PORT all components — regenerate spirit, signal-spirit, meta-signal-spirit and the triad through the emitter, swapping generated Rust in for hand-wired boilerplate; only abstract engine bodies (decide, validate) stay hand-written. Each plane is defined in schema, assembled into Asschema, emitted as Rust datatypes and basic trait impls, with hand-written Rust the decision-making actor system over schema-emitted nouns. Spirit must be a proper contract-daemon/engine-stack exemplar, not an all-in-one non-copyable pilot. Shipped runtime binaries stay small carrying only strict rkyv wire/storage contracts; the schema/NOTA compiler (nota-next, schema-next, schema-rust-next) is build-time-only and never links into the runtime binary, and the NOTA text codec is an optional edge feature absent from the daemon.
survivors.reason: All concern the codegen-is-closed / integrate-and-port phase, Spirit as exemplar, and the build-time-only compiler with small runtime binaries.

survivors.desc: persona-spirit is the production Spirit component (its own repo, currently the only component in production); the repo named spirit (formerly spirit-next) is the next-generation schema-derived Spirit — the active schema-derived implementation should carry the spirit name, since the separate spirit-next repo only arose from the psyche referring to the next Spirit. Future Spirit expansion uses spirit-next as design inspiration while tying together the richer data in production Spirit. Schema work has its own repo named schema. The -next replacement repos have won: nota-next is the canonical NOTA and schema-next + schema-rust-next the canonical schema stack, deprecating the old nota/schema repos; the now-vestigial -next suffix should be dropped, renaming replacements to base names.
survivors.reason: All concern persona-spirit-vs-spirit naming, the schema-repo name, and the -next-repos-won / drop-the-suffix decision.

survivors.desc: Router owns a standardized routing protocol: a router-typed envelope carries routing/object metadata for a serialized contract-owned rkyv payload while Router stays payload-blind beyond envelope/routing/authentication/delivery policy, and is the sole operational subscription matcher for non-direct message passing. Object-update fan-out is owned by subscribing components and Router, not by criome computing per-object impact: criome stamps/authorizes the object and emits an authorized-object reference (not payload); Router matches subscriptions and fans references to subscribers, who fetch the referenced rkyv object. Accepted objects are quorum-backed by default, not single-node signed. Time-driven pulses are not an ambient heartbeat: an after-time contract schedules a later check that, if its events did not occur, triggers a new quorum-signed acceptance. The first production milestone is the spirit -> criome -> router -> mirror chain: Spirit asks local criome to authenticate a content-addressed object, criome verifies/signs it under a cluster-root identity and propagates via Router; the remote mirror fetches/restores object state — criome auth-only, Router transport-only, mirror the version-control substrate. Mirror topology is per-system (one mirror per node, symmetric with the per-system router); the receiving mirror owns fetch/restore after the authenticated head notice. The mirror is the psyche's cross-machine self: editing on any owner machine propagates as a criome-authorized state transition, with criome authority on the latest approved head and Spirit fetching it rather than deciding locally. The mirror aspect may fold into the Spirit daemon.
survivors.reason: All describe the router/criome/mirror/spirit propagation chain: routing protocol, fan-out ownership, object-update pulse, quorum-backed default, time-driven pulses, the production chain, mirror topology, cross-machine self.

survivors.desc: Telos is the umbrella name for the whole meta-project, from Greek telos (ultimate end), whose far horizon is eventual Criome and eventual Sema, realized now as Persona, the meta-AI of language models over typed dumb mechanism. criome is Telos's agreement-and-authorization organ — a universal agreement machine for authorization — not Telos itself. The quorum is criome's universal primitive: one quorum primitive scoped by membership serves both reliability/credible-time (a principal's own multiple nodes) and cross-party trust (multiple principals). criome's internal language is a limited typed policy language over public-key identity atoms (not a general VM; discipline drawn from Ethereum/Tezos/Solana VMs), composing identity contracts from k-of-n signature quorums and time-locks with time-varying thresholds, plus explicit divergence-reconciliation objects (conflict resolution may invoke an LLM-oracle). Verdicts come from gathered BLS quorum signatures, from a connected approver over criome's meta socket (mentci is that client), or from a configured auto-approve policy (bootstrap/testing); contracts can escalate to a named adjudicator (mechanical quorum, LLM panel, smarter agent, or the psyche) whose signed content-addressed verdict criome only verifies. Verdicts are closed typed outcomes over already-submitted objects (approve/reject/defer/non-judge/escalate), never carriers of new content; non-judgment is first-class and the default LLM judge leans toward abstaining and escalating, the psyche being highest-authority lowest-availability (EscalateToPsyche is an inert dead-letter until the psyche UI exists). criome may run as multiple deployment instances (system criome and home/user criome) — same two contracts, differing by privilege tier. The Criome domain component is domain-criome and excludes provider APIs and direct CLI store access. signal-standard is a new shared schema for genuine cross-component standards — first the ComponentKind roster + differentiator (closed-but-partitioned), then standard typed connection points (Unix socket path, optional port); it is the second shared signal- library alongside signal-frame.
survivors.reason: All describe Telos/criome: the meta-project name, criome's role, quorum primitive, policy language, verdict sources/adjudication ladder, deployment instances, domain-criome, and signal-standard.

survivors.desc: criome's clock is decentralized quorum-attested coarse crystallized-PAST time, not a single trusted or precise clock. A time attestation begins as a forward-extending window proposition (content-addressed as its identity); a quorum signs that the present falls within the window, closing it at last signature or expiry, crystallizing a non-forgeable monotonic lower bound on now (only the past is provable). The window extends forward so every member can sign; the tolerance gap is bounded below by quorum signing latency, widening under partition. Every quorum-signed object carries an attested moment (a signature is never timeless), so every admission envelope, verdict, agreement fact, and triad input/output is time-bound for freshness/replay reasoning; time-locks compare against the operation's own proof-of-when. AttestedMoment is the self-grounding base case carrying no inner moment, stopping the regress.
survivors.reason: Distinct dense arrow on criome's crystallized-past time model; kept separate from the broad criome record to preserve the full mechanism.

survivors.desc: CriomOS communication has two transport lanes: Router is the ordinary fabric for cross-sandbox/cross-network traffic (router-to-router remote delivery, reference push, payload-blind byte movement), and criome has a direct criome-to-criome peer lane for time-sensitive agreement (quorum signing, crystallized-time windows) carrying only agreement/authorization messages — keeping criome auth-only and Router general transport. Each agent runs in its own microVM sandbox; the tailnet gives confidentiality and criome BLS attestations per-frame authenticity; intra-host sandbox-to-router transport is tap/L3 (vsock deferred for losing SO_PEERCRED). Mirror version-controls and moves objects.
survivors.reason: Unique: the two-transport-lane CriomOS comms architecture with sandboxing/tailnet details.

survivors.desc: Component binary naming: each component (role name, e.g. persona, spirit, harness, orchestrator) has a CLI half <component> and a daemon half <component>-daemon — the role name is not itself a binary. Persona is composed of binary 'persona' (thin CLI) and 'persona-daemon' (long-lived daemon); converge older phrasings ('persona engine', 'engine-manager daemon') to 'Persona'. Persona disambiguation suffixes only when wrapping (persona-codex, persona-pi, persona-claude). In the running-orchestrated-system goal, Orchestrate is the orchestrate COMPONENT (repo /git/github.com/LiGoldragon/orchestrate), the runtime that runs the set of component daemons (persona the supervisor, the introspector, the schema daemon, others) — not the act of orchestrating.
survivors.reason: All concern component/binary naming convention and the persona+orchestrate role/runtime clarifications.

survivors.desc: persona-prefix retirement and owner->core terminology: persona-spirit becomes spirit, signal-persona-spirit becomes signal-spirit, owner-signal-persona-spirit becomes core-signal-spirit; the pattern drops the persona prefix where not needed and moves owner terminology to core terminology because core is the privileged control/library layer while signal-* stays the ordinary messaging layer for safer callers/extensions. signal-persona-auth renames to signal-persona-origin.
survivors.reason: All concern the persona-prefix-drop and owner->core rename mapping.

survivors.desc: The agent component is a new abstraction over harness backends — persona-claude/codex/gemini/pi/open-code are its backends — built as an LLM-API-call component making provider HTTP API calls in API style, not an agent-harness front door (harness backends deferred); Router talks to the agent component, not harness directly. DeepSeek-as-library folds into the persona-pi triad (no separate deepseek component). Persona Pi is a real persona-component triad (daemon + signal-persona-pi + meta/owner-signal-persona-pi + thin pi CLI), running inside a terminal-cell for Unix I/O but also talking directly to the harness API (the more accessible logic surface). Mencie, the persona multi-modal UI, is implemented as nexus schemas, one per UI panel.
survivors.reason: All concern the agent component abstraction over harness backends, persona-pi triad, and mencie-as-nexus-schemas.
Unique: harness production shape is one daemon owning multiple instances internally.

survivors.desc: mentci is a first-class component triad and a state-bearing programmable user-interface daemon owning canonical interface state (UI changes because daemon state changed); TUI, CLI, editor, status-bar, popup, email, and agentic clients subscribe and send updates back. It interacts heavily with the local criome for question/approval flows and key decrypt/use (criome owns the key store). Shape: mentci daemon repo (daemon + thin CLI + daemon-local signal/nexus/sema runtime schemas) plus signal-mentci and meta-signal-mentci wire contracts; mentci-lib is the shared app/state-machine library reused by daemon and clients, and mentci-egui is one thin client.
survivors.reason: Unique: full mentci component shape and state-bearing UI-daemon model.

survivors.desc: Horizon splits into RAW horizon — the core typed cluster-data model (NodeService enum, typed facts, arch resolution, name/key-line newtypes, cross-node fan-in, typed validation, secret-binding resolution) — and a separate PRETTY horizon layer that computes in typed Rust (not Nix) the pre-derived variables Nix consumes (behaves-as/gating booleans, resolved magnitudes, lid-switch policy, trust-gated extra-groups); Nix only uses already-derived variables. This split is also a forward-integration seam: keeping raw horizon clean and minimal keeps the door open to promote it into a real component later, while pretty stays the typed derive layer. The split applies ONLY to the new lojix/horizon-next stack; production Stack A horizon stays as-is until cutover. Rationale: spend the cleanliness budget on the durable layer that outlives the bootstrap substrate (Nix, which the future Forge replaces) and let unavoidable glue ugliness live in the throwaway substrate.
survivors.reason: All concern the raw/pretty horizon split, its typed-Rust derivation, new-stack-only timing, forward-integration seam, and cleanliness-budget rationale.

survivors.desc: The LojixOS split is a rename-and-configuration split: the generic OS substrate becomes LojixOS while CriomOS becomes a simple per-deploy configuration crate holding a thin NOTA data tree that lojix precompiles into binary config. Cloud-node OS images live in CriomOS as a minimal CloudNode-species profile, declaratively built with an image builder (not by snapshotting a converted running droplet) and content-sized so a node boots fast on the smallest sensible droplet (multi-tens-of-GB is unacceptable). Per-node configuration is declared in the cluster data (the horizon cluster proposal). The cloud daemon references the image by id through HostPlan image_name and never holds the image definition; CriomOS owns the image definition and provider-format build, cloud owns snapshot-id selection and provisioning.
survivors.reason: All concern LojixOS/CriomOS split and the cloud-node image owned by CriomOS, referenced by id from cloud.

survivors.desc: The cloud component's next active capability is on-demand compute-node provisioning via cloud-hoster provider APIs, beyond the shipped Cloudflare read-only DNS path, targeting easiest-to-spin-up providers first. DigitalOcean is the lead compute provider (superseding Hetzner-lead): primary provisioning target, billing by the second/minute so create-and-destroy cycles cost actual elapsed usage and the billing-hour reuse pool is unnecessary for it. Hetzner remains supported but not lead; its hourly billing still uses a billing-hour-aware reuse pool — an idle recently-created node within its paid window is reused instead of delete-and-recreate, reaped before crossing into a new paid hour (keep-warm 59 min for Hetzner), the larger benefit being latency from skipping create/boot/install.
survivors.reason: All concern cloud provisioning: next capability, DigitalOcean lead, and the Hetzner billing-hour reuse pool.
Unique: criome.net public-domain mapping belongs in cluster config.

survivors.desc: For the CriomOS-home dictation/desktop-audio path, keep a reliable laptop-microphone fallback and avoid a hard DJI hot-loop dependency: prefer the connected DJI Bluetooth microphone through system audio default-source policy rather than a Whisrs-specific wrapper, and reduce/delete brittle DJI/PipeWire policy rather than adding keepalive code. A DJI keep-alive may exist only as a sidecar consumer of the real DJI mic source, keeping it awake without becoming the selected source; do not let the fix grow into special-case code.

Block on a subagent only when the psyche asks or is waiting on nothing else.

survivors.desc: persona-llm-client becomes workspace-native component `agent` (drop persona- prefix), its own triad — agent CLI + agent-daemon + signal-agent + owner-signal-agent — a supervised component others call for pre-configured API agent calls (not a thin wrapper, not subsumed into Pi). Hooks to mind for skills/context (model call + agent = a thinking process); takes typed NOTA directly from the model via prompt examples + validate-and-retry, grammar-constrained when self-hosted.
survivors.reason: All six describe the single `agent` component: naming/origin (m2ri, 3v04), triad shape (7yth), role (oog6), mind hookup (y9c6), NOTA interface (l98v). High-fan-in merge runs long to preserve every distinct arrow.

survivors.desc: Persona Pi is the Codex-advisor harness for Persona, target shape a Codex replacement, shipping packaged Pi extensions. Default config is GPT's latest strong model at maximum/xhigh thinking out of the box (currently GPT-5.5 xhigh, GPT-5.4-mini selectable, roles may override). Spawn is tested against Ghostty / terminal-cell spawning.
survivors.reason: All six concern the Pi harness: identity/purpose (6zci, r4ed), default model config (flqt, opmd), extensions (pumu), spawn testing (c54e).

survivors.desc: Browser control is three-tier: a cloud orchestrator (any agent, e.g. GPT-5.5) instructs a local actor model (e.g. Gemma 4 Flash) on the CriomOS AI node, which reads the browser visually via Chrome DevTools Protocol and acts; the CLI's model calls go to that local node, not an external API. The CLI runs from outside the cloud daemon as a packaged PATH command (no orchestrator triad/RemoteBrowse op); a Playwright MCP path is optional. Use supervised scout mode (scan, report state and next steps, await human decisions before consequential actions) on the human's main Chrome profile when safe.
survivors.reason: All six are the browser-automation arrow: tiering (wvgh), local-node redirect (u275), CLI packaging (bxe9), MCP/delegated paths (7o4q), supervised scout (7hmc), Chrome profile (5g4d). Six-record merge runs long to keep each arrow.

survivors.desc: Spirit's intent gate / guardian runs a strong (open-weight if self-hosted, provider TBD) model — intent is too important to under-resource. Relevance, duplication, contradiction are model-only semantic judgments, so the judge sees the full evidence bundle; relevance is computed by a model once at write time and stored as a referent for cheap scoped reuse, bounded by referent/domain scoping and corpus agglomeration.
survivors.reason: Both govern the guardian/intent-gate model: 7mvx on full-bundle + referent caching, 750r on using a strong model. Rephrased affirmative (judge sees full bundle) rather than bare prohibition.
Distinct arrow: agent self-educates in a domain before submitting intent; guardian is fallback not first read. Kept verbatim.
Distinct arrow: concurrent same-prompt capture handling via lock + async non-blocking + prompt-keyed dedup. Kept verbatim.
Distinct arrow: skill rename/refocus to human-interaction; still describes a live skill's scope. Kept verbatim.

survivors.desc: Spirit pilots Criome-backed operation authorization: a Spirit operation needing auth asks Criome to authorize an exact content-addressed request/log digest. criome authenticates the SUBMITTER (SO_PEERCRED caller resolved to a registered criome Identity), as an after-the-fact, non-blocking, out-of-band attestation binding caller to the per-operation digest; the spirit guardian keeps the full content verdict.
survivors.reason: w2g3 opened the envelope question that 2st7 settled; one arrow.
Distinct owner-socket peercred fail-closed rule.

survivors.desc: Long-lived agents have cryptographic identity tied to their Criome master public key (ephemeral keypair when Criome is absent, transitionally). The public key IS the agent identifier — no separate UID storage; graduated short forms (~3/5/7 chars) are methods derived from the key. Possessing identity is the power to sign opinions; Criome Identity is a SignalCore primitive and the canonical existence-verification surface.
survivors.reason: Five records all about agent cryptographic identity = public key.
Distinct cross-system trust-root admission gate.

survivors.desc: lojix integrates with criome-based authentication as part of production cutover: the lojix deploy daemon's operational credentials and unattended machine identity are custodied and authenticated through criome rather than borrowing the operator's logged-in session (GPG/SSH agent). Builds on agents holding cryptographic identity via criome.
survivors.reason: Distinct lojix machine-identity custody arrow; condensed.

survivors.desc: The criome daemon holds an encrypted multi-key store: a master BLS keypair generated on first run, plus sub-keys. It always has a self-start key — the user home private key, the only one readable by the daemon/owning user, encrypted at rest with home-user or system encryption; other keys are sub-encrypted within the store. The secret never leaves criome; the owner can rotate on suspected leak. Transitional bootstrap until meta-signal-criome carries authenticated key config; hardware key mechanism is a later refinement.
survivors.reason: q1le explicitly extends psc6 to a managed multi-key store.
Distinct browser-automation visible-session arrow.

survivors.desc: Secrets always land in the most secure store available: gopass (git-backed, encrypted) in the user session; SOPS-nix (encrypted at rest, decrypted only on target host at activation) for cluster deploy. Move secrets ciphertext-to-ciphertext, never through a visible intermediate. Agents may inspect plaintext only when a psyche/task-authorized job needs it; keep plaintext out of chat, reports, logs, commits, argv, shell traces, Nix store, fixtures, and checked-in files — prefer blind pipe-to-sink, else minimize exposure and move directly to the requested consumer/encrypted store. The local LLM API token installs via a command-backed gopass reference (e.g. Pi auth.json running gopass at call time), never a literal key in Nix source or models.json. Workspace skills must state this discipline so every agent applies it identically.
survivors.reason: Four records all expressing the never-expose-plaintext / most-secure-store secret discipline.

survivors.desc: Secret paths are scoped to the resource they serve: local-service tokens are scoped to the zone where the service lives (distinct from provider-scoped paths like cloudflare/api-token that are global to the provider). The local LLM API token's stable gopass path is goldragon.criome/local-llm-api-token — zone-scoped not host-scoped — so it survives the largeAI role moving between hosts.
survivors.reason: go41 is the concrete instance of ravc's scoping principle.
Distinct prometheus API-auth token mechanism.
Distinct Playwright extension token path.
Distinct cloudflare flarectl gopass shim.

survivors.desc: The backup WiFi password is a human-readable randomly generated secret stored through sops-nix rather than plaintext source or logs.
survivors.reason: Distinct backup WiFi secret; de-negated.

survivors.desc: Cloud credential custody moves toward system-level credentials: the current wire-supplied CredentialHandle the cloud daemon resolves to a process env var behind the 0o600 owner socket is the accepted transitional shape; the eventual direction is system-custodied machine credentials following the criome-custodied machine-identity pattern rather than a wire-selected env var name.
survivors.reason: Distinct cloud credential-custody direction.
Distinct major-bump psyche-authorization gate.

survivors.desc: Version magnitude follows the digits: a small change bumps the patch digit (0.3.0→0.3.1), a functionality change such as privacy work bumps the minor (0.3→0.4). Any component code or logic change bumps the version at least at patch level so deployed binaries and reports never carry stale version numbers after behavioral changes.
survivors.reason: a7pe (bump on change) and rg2i (which digit) are one semver-discipline arrow.

survivors.desc: Horizon/lojix rewrite charter: Horizon stays the simple projection surface (a for-now hack), NOT a full triad component; lojix is the traditional component receiving the full triad-engine and schema-based-component port and carries the runtime triad. Goal: finish the lean rewrite to cutover, retire the dual Stack A/Stack B deploy burden, reach parity then switch over per node. Port high-confidence production CriomOS changes into next lojix/Horizon immediately where the correct change is clear, then test those builds.
survivors.reason: Load-bearing rewrite charter; condensed.

survivors.desc: Component upgrade substrate is Nix-flake versions, universal to every component: each component's flake captures running-production (last known-working main, pinned), local in-development, and named variants (unstable, testing) as inputs; all upgrade sequences are expressed in this flake-input-as-deployed-version structure, and a release tags the whole dependency surface it uses. Tests run through Nix, so commit-first — uncommitted local state is invisible to the evaluator. The endgame is a workspace-owned content-addressed vertical stack: our own content-addressed store replaces Nix store-signing, composing Criome (auth), forge (build, replacing Nix builders), and sema-upgrade (DB migration) so every layer from build to authentication to distribution is in the persona vocabulary.
survivors.reason: 88eq, muf2, p8gv all describe the flake-version upgrade/release substrate and its content-addressed endgame.
Distinct agent-harness lockstep-update rule.

survivors.desc: Multi-version daemon coexistence via version-suffixed names: persona daemons of different versions run side-by-side on one machine, sockets and CLI binaries named by version (e.g. spirit-v0.1.1), with a shim/alias resolving plain unsuffixed names (spirit) to the environment's current default version. Exact shim mechanism is system-specialist territory.
survivors.reason: Distinct multi-version coexistence design; condensed.

survivors.desc: Sema-engine is the exclusive database boundary: all durable state goes through sema-engine (the SEMA plane that writes to the DB file, currently redb), and no component daemon makes direct redb calls — components are storage-dumb and speak only sema-engine operations. SEMA is a single-writer actor (queries, writes, mutations, additions, upgrades) while reads run parallel over redb MVCC. Component DB files use a .sema extension hiding redb so the name declares its architectural role. For the lojix cutover, build durable DB backing now — daemon live-generation-set, GC-roots, event-log persisted with self-resume on restart — rather than first cutover on in-memory state (durable-first). Internal SEMA work is itself a schema language of messages/commands/responses; a component may keep storage in-daemon first and split it out later using the same language. When schema-derived components expose a reusable storage need, improve the shared engine surface to express their real storage identity rather than porting to a mismatched API. The schema component holds the COMPILED binary runtime schema (packed, short-header O(1) lookup, pre-computed version-diff/namespace tables) while NOTA is the source authoring format. Raw mirror/handover payloads stay in a separate container outside the typed DB.
survivors.reason: Nine persistence records all circle the sema-engine DB boundary, single-writer, .sema file, and durable storage architecture.
Distinct tiny daemon-stamped-timestamp fact.

survivors.desc: Component data lifecycle prefers explicit archival lowering before hard deletion: stale or low-certainty data is marked and moved out of the hot working DB to keep runtime state manageable, recoverability best-effort unless a stronger retention class applies. When a record moves to the archive its privacy variable moves with it, preserved at its original privacy level, and archive reads honor the same explicit privacy discipline as the live store.
survivors.reason: Both about archive lifecycle and what archival preserves.

survivors.desc: Zero consumers does not mean a repo is dead: triad signal-component and meta-signal-component contract repos are MEANT to be used — if a component still emits its contract in-repo rather than depending on its standalone signal-X, the correct action is to adopt and wire it, not delete it (a migration gap, not a tombstone). A repository audit must distinguish intended-but-not-yet-adopted from genuinely-dead by understanding the intended component/triad architecture first.
survivors.reason: Durable audit principle distinguishing unadopted contracts from dead repos.

survivors.desc: When the cloud component is built with the cloudflare feature, flarectl is a runtime dependency: cloud binaries are wrapped via makeWrapper/wrapProgram so flarectl is guaranteed on the daemon PATH at runtime, not relying on the user profile.
survivors.reason: Distinct runtime-dependency wrapping rule.

survivors.desc: Agents using Spirit must track the DEPLOYED version's wire interface, not current persona-spirit source: the deployed pinning is reachable through the CriomOS Home flake input — use Nix metadata commands (flake metadata, derivation show, path-info) to find the pinned commit, then read the wire contract at that revision. Operator changes to persona-spirit and its signal contracts drift main from production until the next CriomOS rebuild.
survivors.reason: Distinct deployed-vs-source-version discipline.

survivors.desc: The orchestrate component must be kept on the current triad, signal, sema, and runtime crate set; updating the component dependency surface, not compatibility-helper lockfile work, is the path.
survivors.reason: Distinct orchestrate dependency-surface correction; de-negated.

survivors.desc: Agents should surface dependency relationships routinely — who depends on what, what each repo/component uses and is used by — especially in engine audits and thorough reports, so the psyche keeps a running sense of what is used by what across many repositories.
survivors.reason: Distinct reporting-practice principle.

survivors.desc: Tooling and extensions are packaged declaratively through Nix, hiding Nix paths from internal views: Pi extension sources go in flake inputs with hashes in flake.lock and persona-Pi packages standard Pi extensions through Nix; CriomOS-home packages browser-use with a wrapper using the local Gemma model served by the large-AI node when present; video-editing agents get most normal video-editing tools through the regular medium profile (exceptions for unusually large dependencies); and a Nix utility fetches Hugging Face models by URL/query mirroring nix-prefetch-url (prefetch via the huggingface CLI, hash, write a fetcher derivation parameterised by the HF URL/query) as a workspace tool for getting any HF model into the store.
survivors.reason: Six packaging records all express the Nix-declarative-packaging arrow for various tools.
Distinct dev-input-points-at-branch rule.
Distinct intent-single-source-of-truth migration to Spirit.
Distinct no-reuse-of-identifiers rule.
Distinct meta-report-directory structure (matches AGENTS.md).

survivors.desc: Agent-authored content is not psyche-authorized design surface: distinguish psyche-authorized direction (cite the intent record) from agent-drifted content. Retracting agent-drift restores reality rather than invalidating decided design, so framing such retraction as invalidating substantial design surface mis-attributes authority to agent output.
survivors.reason: Distinct authority-attribution principle.

survivors.desc: Spirit exposes a single guarded CollectRemovalCandidates Signal-root operation that collects all Zero-certainty records (the supersession removal marker), archives them, then retracts them in one call with archive-first safety — if archiving fails nothing is retracted and affected candidates return archive-failed. The discovery-versus-destruction separation is preserved inside the one operation (archive preceding retract), matching deployed behavior and o70j which archives before removal.
survivors.reason: jrx5 refines and supersedes 3v3r's operation shape; one arrow.

survivors.desc: Spirit record identity uses a random/opaque hash, not a content-address fingerprint: because records mutate, content-addressing adds computation and little value while implying stable-content semantics that no longer hold.
survivors.reason: Distinct decision on identifier form.
Distinct four-char-minimum code length.

survivors.desc: Spirit needs a real in-place record mutation path so an existing intent record's content is edited rather than removed-and-recreated. Production Zero-certainty review is not deletion; the system exposes an explicit garbage-collection path for removing reviewed Zero-certainty records. Hard deletion is inevitable on a long timeline because storage is finite, so the design treats hard deletion as a deliberate planned decision rather than a forbidden state.
survivors.reason: All three are the record-lifecycle/mutation/deletion arrow.

survivors.desc: Intent capture should be denser and less verbose: durable records preserve the clarified intent without large verbatim blocks that bloat output and become lossy to work with.
survivors.reason: Distinct intent-density principle.

survivors.desc: Build an always-on real-time intent-recording system on Prometheus using a real-time multimodal model (latest Gemma): the recorder always runs so the model carries full surrounding context, and spoken keyword markers ('Beginning of intent' / 'End of intent' and variants) delimit which spans to extract as discrete intent entries. Achievable on current models at negligible compute; triggered by losing an unrecorded spoken design session.
survivors.reason: uh8l (the system) and 2091 (its boundary markers) are one capture system.

survivors.desc: The intent recorder uses voice recognition as the cutoff between recorded-and-processed and ignored audio: it ignores audio not recognised as the psyche by default (filtering out the psyche's wife who shares the residence). If the active microphone passes to the wife, the recorder recognises the voice change and routes her speech to her own dynamically-spawned persona instance — captured intent belongs to her as a distinct psyche with her own intent layer.
survivors.reason: Both about voice-identity routing in the recorder.

survivors.desc: Long-term intent-recording direction: spirit-CLI-driven intent-recording-of-role for all roles, in real-time, with Prometheus running continuous capture; the chat-mediated capture pattern (psyche prompt → agent extracts → spirit-CLI record) gives way over time to direct real-time recording across every role's interaction surface.
survivors.reason: Distinct long-term all-roles capture direction.

survivors.desc: Local AI on Prometheus: install both big Gemma multimodal models — the ~31B dense for max quality and the ~26B MoE (a4b, ~4B active/token) for fast inference — at bf16 with the f16 vision projector, and deploy multiple quantization variants as separate selectable models in the llama-server router, each named by quant in the modelId (e.g. gemma-4-26b-a4b-bf16, a 4-bit variant), sharing one mmproj per base model so the user picks quality vs speed per use. Local Pi and agent sessions can select these prometheus-hosted models as a local provider. Prefer a curated best/latest model per capability over near-duplicates (Prometheus may prefetch large candidates since memory/storage allows and models needn't run simultaneously). Video speech-to-text is tiered: best path on the large-AI node, smaller local STT for weaker machines.
survivors.reason: Five records all about Gemma model deploy/serving/consumption on Prometheus.

survivors.desc: Spirit observation should support intelligent topic retrieval emphasizing recent intent by default while adapting historical depth to topic frequency — high-churn topics stay near the edge, quiet topics reach farther back for a comparable useful result set. Search may grow beyond simple filters toward weighted keyword or recency scoring, potentially using the Nexus language for richer query logic (exploratory direction, not a settled immediate requirement).
survivors.reason: Both about adaptive Spirit search/retrieval.
Distinct guardian decision-log flywheel.

survivors.desc: Agents install software only ephemerally in this workspace — via nix shell or nix run, matching the workspace's ephemeral declarative Nix ethos. No stateful installs: no pip installs, persistent virtualenvs, or downloaded toolchains in home.
survivors.reason: Distinct no-stateful-install rule; affirmative lead.

survivors.desc: The cluster Rust toolchain is the newest nightly, pinned durably through fenix's flake lock (bump fenix to advance Rust cluster-wide) rather than a rolling stable channel guarded by per-crate frozen sha256. Per-crate flakes route the toolchain through shared rust-build, whose fromToolchainFile yields fenix complete nightly (all components, no hash); a crate gets nightly by repinning rust-build.
survivors.reason: Distinct Rust toolchain pinning decision.

survivors.desc: Agents must not share one jj working copy while concurrently advancing the shared main bookmark — that forks commits off-main and clobbers uncommitted files between write and commit. The workspace needs per-agent jj workspaces (separate working copies via jj workspace add) and single-writer-to-main discipline so only an integrator advances main, including for reports.
survivors.reason: Distinct per-agent-workspace discipline.

survivors.desc: Daemon configuration bootstrap: a daemon's single startup argument is a pre-generated signal-encoded (rkyv) Configure message, not NOTA (a deploy helper or CLI authors NOTA and encodes it). Bootstrap depends on no manager — self-sufficient at boot. The daemon opens the store named in the Configure: if virgin/empty it applies the Configure as first configuration; if already populated it self-resumes from the persisted store. The same Configure type is accepted live over the meta socket for runtime reconfiguration, so bootstrap-config and runtime-config are one vocabulary. Configure is the baseline meta operation of every meta-signal-component contract — the foundational meta content on which component-specific privileged runtime actions build, consistent with binary-only startup (never flags).
survivors.reason: ur16 (bootstrap Configure model) and t803 (Configure is the meta baseline) are one daemon-config arrow.

survivors.desc: Schema supports inline declaration and file-path references (paths resolved into the full spec before macro processing). Schema node values lower by context; bare identifiers resolve to namespace objects.

survivors.desc: Component code-generation direction (designed/prototyped, not integrated): the schema generates a component's Rust by EXPANDING bound universal frames into concrete owned interfaces, not persisting generic aliases. Goal is narrow — generate repeated interface boilerplate: Input/Output and work/action enums, payload structs/newtypes/enums, standard inherent/trait impls (constructors, accessors, From legs, scalar-newtype Display/AsRef/comparisons), role/marker trait impls, an opt-in mechanical Deref the newtype shape proves, and later a VariantMatch for enum-rewrap. Traits/impls are the pipe-brace construct expressing Rust relationships as data so hand-written engine code accepts generated nouns through typed bounds. Method bodies are data ONLY for the small mechanical family the schema shape uniquely proves (newtype Deref projection); genuine business logic stays hand-written. The durable principle: a schema-authored body is valid only when every call resolves from the receiver's schema shape. Open pieces: generic impl-header emission with parameter-bound vocabulary, fn/signature sub-construct for trait declarations, ownership-aware payload-vs-into_payload projection, total VariantMatch. Settled constraints and the open how live in the schema-next and nota-next architecture files.

survivors.desc: Keep working productively without stalling: do not block synchronously waiting for a dispatched subagent — run other work (own thinking, tool calls, context reading, chat prep) while it is in flight and check the result at a natural integration point, with multiple tools in flight. Mid-task info the psyche injects (a report, clarification, context — often via escape) is NOT an instruction to stop; absorb it and keep executing; only an explicit stop is an interruption. Treat transient connection warnings as non-blocking when tooling still works and resume the assigned job rather than detouring into debugging. Beads are coordination aids, not a substitute for implementation — work the bead/code path immediately unless blocked. In active troubleshooting where the user asked for a working result, apply a narrow safe fix rather than stopping at diagnosis. After subagent prototype work, run context maintenance and a fresh-intent audit over recent reports/code, then synthesize and implement sensible immediate fixes rather than letting stale context drive the next slice.

When work surfaces many open psyche questions, lean on pattern-recognition of captured intent and propose an MVP matching the letter of intent, surfacing only the truly-unanswerable remainder rather than dozens of inferable questions.

The split is about compilation/dependency isolation and authority clarity, not where state or logic lives.

survivors.desc: A content/data daemon's privilege boundary is can-ingest-and-serve-content, not can-read-anything-on-the-system: it is fairly privileged (ingesting/serving content is not especially dangerous) but never absolutely privileged. Its store stays unwritable by anything except the daemon itself — even root writing it is system misbehavior. It has no ambient access to system private-key material and, handed a path to a private key, must be unable to read it.
survivors.reason: Three records define the content-daemon privilege boundary.

survivors.desc: Each agent has its own Criome identity, created at agent start, recorded in persona-mind; the agent's Criome public key is the authoritative authentication anchor. Complex (especially agent-initiated) Criome interactions run through the agent's own Criome daemon, and authorization is checked against Criome rather than SSH for many workspace operations — every component/daemon can escalate to Criome for an authorization check. The substrate is per-component-per-domain agent spaces. criome is per-Unix-user: each user runs their own home criome daemon with their own key custody, the Unix-user boundary being the trust boundary; there is no single shared multi-user system daemon (that would collapse every user behind one compromise point). The privileged system criome tier is for host-scoped system services under a service user that a home criome peers with, not a shared daemon — the same single-Unix-user custody boundary that rules out an embeddable criome engine library.

survivors.desc: NOTA has two bracket-string forms: [content] inline-bracket-string for single-line strings; [|content\|] block-string for multi-line strings and for strings safely containing single [ or ] without escaping (delimited [| / \|] so content may include [, ], or newlines freely). Both are distinct NOTA shapes the shape-logic layer distinguishes via is_block_string and the underlying parser.
survivors.reason: Distinct NOTA bracket-string forms.

survivors.desc: The schema reader is multi-pass NOTA-first: one universal NOTA parser produces a generic NotaValue tree, then context-sensitive macro passes interpret values per their NodeDefinitionPoint position, then assembly produces canonical schema — one syntax (NOTA), one parser, multiple semantic passes. NOTA parsing breaks down by object via positional ranges: each block tracks its source span and carries shape predicates (is_square_bracket_block / is_parenthesis_block / is_brace_block; holds_single/two/N_root_objects; recursive predicates like second_root_object_is_a_square_bracket_object), and each root object inside a block is itself a typed/positioned block, so the recursive parsing chain IS the chain of block-level queries. Schema parsing first identifies NOTA delimiters and their roles (parens for enum/variant, square brackets for vector-like sequences and string-derived token vectors, curly braces for name-value namespace maps), then runs passes per source block: pass 1 finds identifier uses and locations, pass 2 registers inline declarations into the namespace and resolves references by lookup — supporting bare-PascalCase-or-explicit-Variant-Payload shorthand, with the source representation (not lowered fields) telling whether each identifier defines, references, or both. NotaBodyDecode is the semantic decode entry point: document/block decode strip their wrapper context and delegate to the body decoder. After structural parsing matches a file body or delimited object, the next semantic step receives the matched body's inner object stream rather than the outer delimiter wrapper, and the expected type decides whether the body reads as struct, vector, enum variant, or other value. Schema source needs its own typed node model before assembled schema — a streamlined tree of typed structural-macro nodes, namespaces, paths, fields, variants, references representing syntax directly (including inline PascalCase local declarations and self-named field shorthand) — and schema can act as its own codec so shorthand round-trips as typed data rather than being a one-way parser convenience. Block reassembly concatenates blocks one-after-another; parsed blocks are first-class units re-emitted in order without nesting reconstruction.
survivors.reason: Nine parsing records all describe the multi-pass NOTA-first schema parser, its block model, and the typed source node model.

survivors.desc: A daemon binary compiles with no NOTA code at all: NOTA encode/decode is a thin-CLI text-edge concern only, the daemon speaks binary/rkyv exclusively and must not pull nota-next into its artifact. Prefer plain English over jargon: say what is blocked, why it matters, and what decision or action is needed. Append line-number suffixes (:1) to file paths only when the psyche asks.
survivors.reason: Merge: all four govern plain-language psyche communication and identifier/reference hygiene; over-280 retained because each rule is a distinct arrow.
Distinct: word rules positively, naming the desired practice.

survivors.desc: Introduce an archive system as a specialized sema-database holding exactly one kind of archived object, the database name stating what it archives, paired with a small archive-retrieving tool. When composite intent retires its source records, the sources are archived and referenced by hash identity from the composite so provenance survives cleanly. CollectRemovalCandidates archives collected records automatically to a default archive surface before hot-store removal, with typed retrieval rather than ad-hoc caller files.
survivors.reason: Merge: all three describe the archive system and provenance-preserving removal.

survivors.desc: Persona-Pi acceptance includes live terminal usability and readable themes; the default Pi profile is the fully-featured basic experience, and Pi exposes both terminal-cell and harness API surfaces.
survivors.reason: Merge: all three describe Persona-Pi surface/profile expectations.
Distinct, Maximum importance: Spirit categories are an open curated vocabulary of hundreds of grounded domains.

survivors.desc: The intent layer holds intent — what the psyche directs, decides, or wants — not information or belief; a durable-sounding statement with no directive behind it is information, not intent, and is not captured. When a design surface is incomplete, agents ask the psyche rather than generating plausible synthesis and capturing it as psyche-authorized; inferring to close the loop produces fake hallucinated records.
survivors.reason: Merge: both define what is/isn't real intent and warn against fabricated intent.

survivors.desc: One consistent cryptographic basis spans the entire version-control and backup system: blake3 for all content addressing and Criome BLS (BLS12-381 curve, BLS signature scheme) for signing and attesting history; no component diverges. The pre-Criome agent-identity path uses BLS12-381 keypairs (not Ed25519) from day one so identity transitions cleanly when Criome lands.
survivors.reason: Merge: both establish the single blake3+BLS crypto basis.

Distinct: no backslash-n escapes in single-line NOTA; use spaces or actual newlines.
Distinct: prioritize Spirit + one or two components working excellently before spreading effort.
Distinct: ask fewer questions at a time with enough context.

Audit-cycle working procedure (refresh intent, re-audit from latest operator main, re-present good prior worktrees) now living in the designer protocol / INTENT.md; transient operational detail, not durable distinct intent.

survivors.desc: Tracing is a schema-defined interface with closed generated enum vocabularies for trace names and events, not an ad-hoc string log. Trace names are macro-emitted from the schema enum variant structure, which already owns the activated object's identifier; instrumentation records only that object name, not rich per-boundary payload snapshots.
survivors.reason: Merges the core typed-tracing arrow: schema-defined enum vocabulary, macro-emitted names from variant structure, and record-only-object-name. nq28 and t1da are refinements of m5jl's central decision.

survivors.desc: Trace events render as NOTA at the client edge: trace event types derive a NOTA codec from their schema definitions, so display reduces to the standard NOTA encoder, not ad-hoc Display formatting — the rendered string IS NOTA text, the typed-data text projection.
survivors.reason: Distinct arrow on client-edge rendering via derived NOTA codec. Condensed from mini-essay, kept the concrete mechanism.

survivors.desc: Tracing belongs in the schema-generated engine traits themselves via default derived/no-op trait implementations, so the trace hooks live on the interface/actor contract and trace builds override or install a sink — not as separate SignalTrace, NexusTrace, SemaTrace side traits.
survivors.reason: Distinct architectural arrow: trace hooks belong to generated engine traits, not parallel side traits. De-narrated.

survivors.desc: The schema-derived runtime supports an optional testing/instrumentation build surface where generated objects and engine interface usage emit structured trace events to a logging socket, proving actual runtime use of Signal, Nexus, and SEMA interfaces during tests rather than mere symbol existence.

survivors.desc: A trace-client library in the repo owns the display plus SEMA-log features; the CLI is a thin wrapper enabling and calling those features rather than reimplementing listener/decoder logic per component. Forge-core is the shared standardization contract sub-forges adopt as they mature. survivors.reason: Both about synthetic firmware gating: 6wz8 avoid unfree firmware, fyuz stay generic + Nix-verified. One arrow.

survivors.desc: Persona is the live runtime manager: it supervises persona-introspect, the schema daemon, and triad components as one orchestrated whole, and manages component upgrades via systemd template units (persona-component@<component>:<version>.service; UnitController trait, systemd-D-Bus in prod, direct-fork in tests). Atomic no-downtime version swaps mean CriomOS needs no per-component redeploy. Upgrade is drain-with-mirror: acked==durable, mirror transfers the state snapshot, in-flight ops drain, atomic socket handoff. Cutover is lossless and client-transparent over one stable socket.
survivors.reason: Six records describe Persona-supervised live runtime and its atomic no-downtime upgrade: systemd template units, drain-with-mirror, lossless client-transparent cutover, root persona owning orchestration. One arrow. Tightened to ~280 chars (was ~700); arrows preserved.

survivors.desc: Lojix is the production deploy path: execute the cutover now — the lojix daemon becomes production and legacy lojix-cli retires per node, standing down the parallel Stack A / Stack B burden (piloted at parity on ouranos). A lojix deploy/build is durable: a job actor owns the external process and persists state, surviving client disconnect; cancellation is declared per operation (durable deploys survive disconnect by default, speculative queries/builds may opt into kill-on-drop). Keep using lojix while making the workload safe; the risk is local build/eval resource pressure. Sandbox passing precedes the lean-stack cutover to main.
survivors.reason: Four records on lojix as production deploy path: cutover, durable job-actor semantics, lojix-safe-to-keep, sandbox precondition. One arrow.

Distinct decision: lojix-daemon owns GitHub-authenticated Nix flake input resolution via a token-injection library (gopass key into NIX_CONFIG access-tokens). Specific scope expansion not covered elsewhere.

survivors.desc: Node NixOS configs build and realize on the target node itself (or its configured remote builder); coordinating machines evaluate only — realizing a model-bearing closure elsewhere drags tens-of-GB models onto the wrong store (extends heavy-work-placement rule 0a9p to all node builds). Large-AI model prefetches and fixed-output hash discovery run on the node (e.g. prometheus) over its wired LAN as durable target-side systemd work with a target-side periodic monitor, surviving SSH drops. Quantized variants install through the CriomOS large-AI model inventory deploy path; disruptive promotions launch as durable systemd transient units. Deploy uses lojix-cli on the host.
survivors.reason: Heavy-work-placement and durable-target-side-work cluster: builds on the node, model inventory deploy path, durable systemd prefetch/promotion surviving SSH drops over wired LAN. One arrow.

survivors.desc: Deploying to the large-AI node (CriomOS role large-ai-node, NodeService::LargeAi — today prometheus, running hostapd and dnsmasq) must preserve its network connectivity: a live switch restarts those services and drops the connection (broke the gemma deploy). Use the safe BootOnce / boot-mode activation path rather than Switch until console/out-of-band access and sign-off exist, and avoid staging an unverified generation for an unintended reboot. Name the role not the host — refresh existing Prometheus references that should be role-level. FullOS deploys update the production CriomOS flake lock before deploying Zeus.
survivors.reason: Router-node deploy safety: preserve connectivity on the providing node, prefer BootOnce, name the role not the host. kx32 (flake-lock-before-Zeus) folded into safe FullOS sequencing.

Pi operator-safety proceeds without asking merely because a repository is dirty.

survivors.desc: Three schema types for three runtime planes — Signal (wire), Nexus (execution IO/UI), SEMA (durable state). Each plane has its own engine and traits but shares one pattern: run code over an input message, return an output message. Rust emission splits the planes into separate namespaces so payloads use plain names (Input, Output) without plane prefixes.
survivors.reason: Three records all describe the three-plane model and its emitted namespacing; one unified arrow.
Distinct arrow: root type is the message surface; each schema declares its own input/output enums with colon-path imports.

survivors.desc: Schema-to-Rust lowering is token-based via Rust's real macro infra (quote!/proc-macro2 TokenStream/ToTokens), not a hand-rolled string emitter or god-struct writer. Each schema noun (RustStruct, RustEnum, RustField, TypeExpression) renders itself recursively; cross-object concerns (plane split/projections) live in a PlaneType the nouns consult. Checked-in .rs vs inline expansion is a separate visibility choice.
survivors.reason: Both records express one arrow: methods-on-nouns token-based lowering replacing the RustWriter god-struct. de8i is explicitly unified with 4np2 in its own text.

survivors.desc: Every component derives two vocabularies from one .schema file: an internal effect language for engine-internal actors over each other and storage (actor mediator surface), and an external wire language for signal contracts via signal sockets (signal-frame envelope, ShortHeader, dispatch). The signal_channel macro emits all three: wire surface, sema operations, and sema lowering operations.
survivors.reason: Both describe the effect-vs-wire dual derivation from one schema and what the macro emits; one arrow.

survivors.desc: NOTA encode/decode derives are optional generated surfaces, applied only where needed; daemon-only components stay binary-only (rkyv signal frames) and carry no NOTA decoding code.
