survivors.desc: Schema macro expansion ends in AssembledSchema (not SchemaMid): a pure NOTA-representable assembled-file type with fully resolved enum/struct definitions, typed endings, no remaining macros. It is the complete input to Rust generation — including NOTA text and portable rkyv binary for SEMA and signal formats — and may later be driven from a single macro reading repo schema files.
survivors.reason: Three records all define AssembledSchema (its shape, its naming correction, its role as generation input). One arrow.

survivors.desc: Rust emission goal: a single macro in lib.rs/main.rs reads the repo's .schema files and generates ALL schema-derived Rust — data types plus NOTA and rkyv read/write — as the sole derivation entry point. The signal-to-sema engine (Spirit full-schema branch) carries per-turn query and effect-to-response schemas, participating in this same path.
survivors.reason: Both describe the single-macro entry point and what participates in the generation path.

survivors.desc: Generated Rust is one module per .schema file, mirroring schema layout on disk so name clashes namespace per module. Separator is a single colon mirroring crate:module:type (crate name from Cargo). Each crate has a schema folder with lib.schema entry; emission targets src/schema/ beside hand-written Rust, re-emitting automatically with watch-hook hot-reload; placement stays pragmatic, interfaces schema-derived.
survivors.reason: Five records all about module-per-schema-file layout, colon-path mirroring, src/schema emission, hot-reload, and pragmatic placement. One arrow. Compressed but every distinct sub-arrow kept.

survivors.desc: Lowered output uses fully-qualified universal identifiers (crate::module::type) so cross-component collisions are impossible and the form self-documents for verification and machines; the sugar form is for authoring, both equivalent. Imports clashing with a local name error rather than shadow. Schema and Rust mirror by naming, so a colon-path like spirit-next:signal:Frame greps from either side.
survivors.reason: Three records on fully-qualified naming, self-documentation, and the schema<->Rust naming mirror. One arrow.

survivors.desc: Generated and handwritten Rust prefers real data-bearing types and trait/associated-type bindings over transparent aliases. Bare namespace bindings (Rejected SignalRejection) lower to TypeDeclaration::Alias (pub type), not newtypes; genuine/unnamed single-field bodies stay transparent newtypes (only multi-field structs get field names). Generated APIs spare callers nested wrapper construction, which signals a missing emission layer.
survivors.reason: Five records all about alias/newtype lowering, avoiding transparent aliases, and not forcing nested wrapper construction. One tightly-related arrow.

survivors.desc: Schema source carries the triad engine mechanism as baseline — runner shape, trace plumbing, continuation substrate by generation. Authors implement the three plane engines plus effect handler and budget-exhausted reply; the generator bundles those as glue for the shared runner. Generated traits carry minimal lifecycle hooks (on_start/on_stop); the emitter evolves to host lojix load-bearing daemon properties (bounded per-request workers over a shared store, typed owner meta tier, frame/timeout hardening). Per-component variation uses explicit escape hatches.
survivors.reason: Four records on the generated triad runner/runtime: bundled glue, lifecycle hooks, baseline mechanism with escape hatches, and lojix daemon properties feeding the emitter. One arrow. Compressed but all four sub-arrows kept.

survivors.desc: Every root enum emitted by schema-rust-next gets an auto-generated Help action variant sourced from the schema interface description; help responses are themselves generated root-shape enums carrying their own Help variant (recursive). Self-description is part of every component's typed interface contract, projected from schema, not a separate doc surface.
survivors.reason: Distinct arrow: auto-generated recursive Help variant as part of the typed contract.

survivors.desc: Schema-next keeps one lowering engine — the most correct lowering path, not dual paths or the smallest patch — and schema-derived Rust emission targets that engine rather than rewiring into the old signal macro implementation.
survivors.reason: Both about one canonical lowering path and not reverting to the old emitter. De-negated.

survivors.desc: The schema-rust-next emitter consolidates same-shape sibling variants by semantic family: when variants in a closed-sum enum carry identical payload shapes, it groups them under one family-named variant whose body carries the original variant name as data (the common case for rkyv-wrapped closed sums).
survivors.reason: Distinct emitter behavior: same-shape variant consolidation by family.

survivors.desc: AssembledSchema distinguishes exported public data types from local module data types — modeled either as visibility on declarations or as public/local declaration variants — and the compiled Rust preserves the boundary.
survivors.reason: Distinct arrow: public-vs-local type visibility boundary preserved in lowering.

survivors.desc: Schema-stack presentations, tests, and design reports show the schema-to-interface path: each component interface, the schema that creates it, the path to generated interface and component communication, plus the derived REST code, typed interfaces, traits, impls, and derivation boundaries where flaws can be spotted.
survivors.reason: Both about reports/tests/presentations exposing the schema-to-generated-interface path and its boundaries. One arrow.

removed.reason: Settled implementation detail: the VersionProjection macro chooses its own emission module structure without psyche gating. Encapsulated in code now; no durable cross-cutting arrow.

Aspirational direction restatement (move more runtime behavior into macro-generated interface, generate default trait wrappers/hooks). Distinct arrow fully covered by ycmd (engine writes only logic) plus the triad-runner survivor (czw0/rpr5/hyng/tj99: generated traits/lifecycle hooks/bundled glue so authors fill only domain decisions).

survivors.desc: Schema is the programmable composability layer over NOTA: it interprets delimiter-based, quote-free NOTA into typed input-output interfaces for actor interactions. NOTA is a thin structure-sensing library (is_parenthesis, holds_N_objects) carrying no meaning; the schema/macro layer interprets it. Schemas express composable data types and data-driven behavior trees (node-graphs) for Signal, Nexus, SEMA, and sub-engine behavior, and are a reusable declaration bedrock, not only a Spirit contract format. Same NOTA text means different things under different schemas.
survivors.reason: Eight records all state the same core arrow: schema = programmable typed-interface layer interpreting NOTA. Merged the gap-fills, DSL framing, reusable-bedrock decision, and NOTA-as-thin-structure-library decision. Tightened desc.

survivors.desc: Foundational model (supersedes the at-sigil for macro invocation): EVERYTHING is a struct — unit variant = zero-field struct, data variant = one-field struct, enum = single-field struct holding the active variant. A schema node is a tagged variant (paren: first object = tag picking the variant of the KNOWN expected type, second = carried data), read against the schema-of-schemas, so positional typing resolves variant-vs-macro with no sigil. A macro is a Rust struct; macro-space is a tagged union of them; invoking is a tagged variant against the known macro type. The schema is ONE recursive shape: a root struct with macro-expanded fields down to scalar leaves; macros expand structurally by position; a delimiter shape (e.g. bracket field vector) means struct/newtype only at the lowerer's position; recursion terminates at built-in macros over scalar leaves (ints, unit-variant enums, bools, typed-newtype strings).
survivors.reason: Merged the everything-is-a-struct model, one-recursive-shape clarification, vector-of-PascalCase=struct rule, and the shape-means-struct-only-at-lowerer correction. Positional-typing recursive structural model.
Open design tension: enum-vs-macro paren ambiguity may need an explicit discriminator (vs umsv's positional-typing resolution). Distinct unresolved arrow, kept verbatim as it qualifies umsv.

survivors.desc: Schema is a SUPERSET of Cap'n Proto-style spec languages, adding (1) its own module system, (2) a macro system, (3) shape-driven node-type matching (structure + member count + member types resolve a node's type) — a real spec language without becoming general-purpose. Scope: the workspace's Rust subset only, no tuples (tuples are poorly-defined structs). The recursive-parser + dispatch + lowering model is a reusable library for any NOTA DSL (Shen-style mid-compilation assembly); schema is its first application. The aski-core/askic pattern is an analog informing the discipline, not a foundation to replicate.
survivors.reason: Merged the Cap'n-Proto-superset definition, reusable-DSL-library-over-NOTA principle, and aski-core-is-analog-not-foundation correction (gives the Rust-subset/no-tuples scope). Dropped stray lojix referent (not in source).

survivors.desc: The schema language is self-describing: the meta-schema is defined in the schema language's own format and the language is specified by its own .schema file. A schema-library repo holds a bare schema file defining the foundational namespace (Schema, Channel, Operation, Reply, Event, Namespace, TypeName, TypeDeclaration, Path) and bootstraps via its own basic struct format. Schema needs explicit specs for AssembledSchema types and the minimal built-in object, plus a self-description tested as an artifact that lowers through the current engine and exposes the root Schema shape and nested declaration types — even while the bootstrap Rust reader stays hand-written.
survivors.reason: Four records on schema self-description/bootstrap: meta-schema in own format, specified by own file, self-description-as-tested-artifact, explicit specs + bootstrap layer. One arrow.

survivors.desc: Move schema declaration out of Rust macros into a specialized NOTA-format schema language that is the SOURCE of truth: codegen produces (a) Rust types/impls, (b) per-version namespace-slot-assignment tables, (c) version-diff auto-marking types for upgrade-migration on recompile. The Rust macro becomes a consumer of structured schema data, not a parser of Rust syntax; compilation auto-assigns slot numbers and size optimization. NOTA signal schemas are also documented as a class (the wire vocabulary in NOTA form) separately from the binary signal layer. Direction firm; exact shape needs iterative design.
survivors.reason: Both records: NOTA schema is the source, Rust generated from it (macro becomes consumer). km3w adds the doc-as-a-class move and proposal framing.

survivors.desc: Each of the three schema types (Signal, Nexus, SEMA) is its own LANGUAGE sharing the SAME 4-position shape — Imports, Input, Output, Namespace — and the SAME import/export mechanism: a single-colon namespace path mirroring Rust modules (one colon, not two), e.g. signal-frame:Frame or schema:spirit:Entry. The languages differ only in which types and macros fit each position. The namespace position is both export and definitions surface; imports/exports are part of the root structure with conflict checking across imported exports; selective object import and load-all are both supported. Names must resolve from defined namespaces or imports; for the MVP, Spirit may define all types locally and imports may be deferred.
survivors.reason: Six records on the symmetric Imports/Input/Output/Namespace shape and colon-path import/export across the three schema languages, including conflict checking, selective import, and the MVP local-definitions allowance.

survivors.desc: Schema names carry meaning from their definition site and refer to typed declarations elsewhere in the schema or imported from another schema, never free symbols invented at use sites; references may be forward. The namespace is dependency-ordered (basic definitions first, derived later; consumers reference only earlier-declared names) and assembled schema makes names globally unambiguous by context. Imported schema names must not collide in one context.
survivors.reason: Four records on name resolution/ordering/uniqueness in schema namespaces. One naming-discipline arrow.

survivors.desc: Authored-schema declaration forms: only the root object name may be omitted (reader knows the root type); every nested object names itself. An enum definition is a paren of (qualified-name, paren-of-qualified-symbols) and must name its enum type — a bare parenthesized variant list is invalid; a struct is the bracket second-object form, a one-symbol bracket a newtype. Root struct positional fields are bare (no Name@); the Name@Delimiter rule applies only to user declarations of new type names — additional plane roots (NexusInput/Output, SemaInput/Output) declared inside the namespace keep Name@. Inline PascalCase declarations introduce local reusable types when they precede references (left-to-right); camelCase stays field names. Pipe delimiters are generics/traits, not declarations; assembled schema stays legal NOTA. Spirit schema files have no outer root parens, declare header/root first, encode enums as ordered variant vectors, and cover ordinary/owner/Sema headers.
survivors.reason: Six records specifying concrete authored-schema syntax: enum/struct/newtype shapes, root-name omission, bare-positional-fields vs Name@ declarations, inline PascalCase types, pipe=generics, Spirit-file root form. One syntax-rules arrow.

survivors.desc: The structural patterns of the schema substrate (deep typed trees, closed variants, recursive composition, separation of syntax/verification/semantics) emerged FIRST in the psyche's Aski language work — aski-core (rkyv contract types for the askic-veric-semac triad: compiler, verifier, semantics), vscode-aski tooling, and the core parse-tree primitives (body, domain, expression, module, pattern, primitive, program, statement, trait, type). The schema engine is the second iteration of these insights. (Corrects a prior STT artifact mishearing Aski as ASCII.)
survivors.reason: Unique lineage fact: schema's structural insights originated in Aski. Stripped report-correction narration and STT-blame; kept the durable provenance arrow.
Tiny but durable concrete fact (.schema extension) not stated elsewhere. Kept verbatim with referents.

survivors.desc: Bracket semantics: () is enum/variants, [] is struct/fields — a struct is a positional vector of fields ([] vector semantics), an enum is one-of alternatives (() grouping). Reading: Entry [Topic Kind Summary] is a struct; Kind (Decision Principle Correction) is an enum. Container forms like (Vec T) stay on () as a single-tagged-variant form (or [Vec T] under stricter reading — open).
survivors.reason: The bracket-semantics convention ()=enum []=struct is a distinct load-bearing decision. Stripped migration narration; kept the rule and the open container question.

removed.reason: Thin near-empty principle (cloud signal language starts small and typed); subsumed by the start-small-typed spirit of the schema-language arrows; no distinct durable particular.

Schema data should not rely on comments for section structure — a settled minor formatting constraint now implicit in the structural-not-comment-driven schema model; no longer load-bearing on its own.

survivors.desc: DomainScope uses ordinary schema-emitted recursive enum semantics, staying typed all the way through matching, expansion, and equivalence. Generated code never hand-parses path shortcuts, hides stop markers, or degrades values into Debug strings or Vec<String> paths.
survivors.reason: Both records express one arrow: DomainScope must stay typed recursive-enum end to end, no hand-parsing or string degradation.

survivors.desc: The entire @-binder surface is abandoned; nota-next removes all @-binding parser support. The authored schema surface is the positional bracket/brace form ({...} struct bodies, [...] enum/variant lists), which rejects @ at source. The root schema value stays an implicit filename-named struct; legacy pipe declarations are transitional, replaced by the positional form.
survivors.reason: Load-bearing abandonment of @-binding plus the surviving truths it carries from superseded n2z3; central grammar decision. Trimmed parser-path narration; kept all six arrows.
Distinct still-relevant rule on how an optional-empty-payload variant renders.

survivors.desc: Macro application and enum declaration both use the grouped form: a head plus one parenthesized payload, e.g. (Foo (A B)). In an enum declaration Foo is the declared type and A, B are variants; in a macro invocation Foo is the operator applied to grouped arguments A, B.
survivors.reason: Distinct grammar rule on grouped head-plus-payload form; condensed, removed the past-error narration.

survivors.desc: [] is and always has been the vector delimiter in NOTA. Schema and assembled-schema layers may read a vector at a typed position as a product/field list, but [] itself stays a vector and is never redefined as struct syntax.
survivors.reason: Distinct invariant about [] being the vector delimiter; de-negated to affirmative.

survivors.desc: The three NOTA delimiters map to the three schema sections globally: parentheses hold enum/variant headers and choices (generic enum fields and header root endpoints included), square brackets hold positional struct field vectors/bodies, and curly braces hold the name-value namespace of type names to definitions.
survivors.reason: Four records all state the delimiter-to-section correspondence; merged. jqvu (generic enum fields use parens) and ogod (header root endpoints, global rule) preserved inline as enum-choice cases.
Distinct rule on the block-string wrapper for multiline NOTA-like content.

survivors.desc: Plain square brackets remain the desired candidate for inline string literals; the [|...|] block form is too much delimiter for common inline strings and is reserved for the multiline/bracket-safe case.
survivors.reason: Distinct preference on inline string literal form; complements bhs5; de-negated.

survivors.desc: The .schema source uses header-first sections with no outer wrapper, and is not a flat vector of same-kind declarations. Schema root input and output are known positional fields serialized as positional input/output enum bodies, not data-carrying variants labeled (Input ...) / (Output ...).
survivors.reason: Four records describe the schema source shape: no outer wrapper, header-first sections, not a flat declaration vector, and input/output as positional fields not labeled variants. Condensed.

survivors.desc: Schema header variant references resolve via namespace lookup or inline declarations: a bare PascalCase name in Input/Output root bodies looks up its namespace type, an inline declaration at variant position auto-registers it, and root bodies take inline payloads directly (e.g. [Record { Topic * }]). Multi-pass resolution replaces the verbose (Variant Payload) form.
survivors.reason: Both records cover header variant/payload resolution via namespace lookup and inline declarations, eliminating the verbose (Variant Payload) form. Condensed; kept namespace-lookup, auto-register, inline-payload, multi-pass arrows.

survivors.desc: Under the current macro grammar the head symbols in ((Parse Expression) (Render Expression) ...) read as macro/operator heads, not enum variants, so this double-wrapped Input/Output form is denied as schema grammar until a distinct enum-variant context or explicit enum form is established.
survivors.reason: Both reject the double-wrapped Input/Output example and explain the enum-variant-vs-macro-head ambiguity behind the rejection. Reframed affirmatively around how the heads parse.

survivors.desc: Single-type field syntax may be (Type) for an existing defined type, and a single-name enum variant record carries the same named type.
survivors.reason: Two tightly-related clarifications on single-name field/variant shorthand referencing a named type.

removed.reason: Narrow import-header arity rule; settled implementation detail of the schema reader, no longer a live distinct arrow.
Low-certainty speculation about dissolving enum-vs-macro ambiguity by making struct/enum built-in; superseded by the settled positional grammar in own9 and fo38.

Low-certainty grammar exploration of plain-delimiter semantics (map/reference/Optional/newtype); largely subsumed by the settled delimiter-to-section correspondence and positional form. Archived and recoverable if the value-layer claims become live.
Low-certainty exploratory note on schema sigils typing symbols; not a settled load-bearing arrow.
Low-certainty stub clarification that namespace values need a distinct declaration language; subsumed by the settled curly-brace namespace section and esn1 resolution.

survivors.desc: Everything is a serializable data object, the macro most of all: a macro is data with name, position, pattern, and template, where pattern and template are themselves data trees (not text with sigils). Macros pre-assemble, serialize, and load as data; reading a schema loads pre-assembled macro data and interprets schema-data against it. The only code is one tiny generic interpreter; no per-macro bespoke parsing. Test: everything round-trips through NOTA text and rkyv bytes, macros included.
survivors.reason: Foundational macros-as-data anchor. De-narrated the current-engine-violates clause into the affirmative round-trip test; tightened.

survivors.desc: NOTA extension is structural matching over nodes. The structural macro node is a NOTA enum decoded by shape: decode is type-directed, the codec structurally matches each variant in declaration order (most-specific first, first wins), recursively; encode emits a matching NOTA block (bidirectional). Realized as #[derive(StructuralMacroNode)] with per-variant shape attributes, not a runtime registry or string-name dispatch. The raw parser preserves delimiter structure, counts, sigils, and nesting; macros are NOTA-layer typed records (name, structural-match pattern, capture specs, position) dispatched as an ordered list with conflict detection at registry construction. The pattern language is a typed enum of primitives (arity, atom-case, atom-sigil, delimiter-type, atom literal) so macro data stays serializable.
survivors.reason: Merges the core mechanism cluster: structural matching (kchq), type-directed shape codec (xai7), ordered registry with conflict detection (c2dc), typed primitive pattern language (5xss), macro-as-NOTA-record (t2xg). One structural-macro-node arrow. Tightened.

survivors.desc: Define assembled schema (.asschema) before sugar: it is macro-free final data — Vec, Optional, Enum, Struct, Newtype, Plain, Carries are final assembled-schema variants, not macro invocations. Sugar belongs only in authored schema before lowering. Schema macros re-emit schema structure toward AssembledSchema (conceptually re-emitting NOTA, intermediate may stay binary) and must expand into a fully one-to-one assembled tree describing all generated types, endpoint variant names, and carried input structs before Rust is emitted. Application is iterative to a fixed point: each pass applies lowerers at macro positions, macros may introduce new macros, until the namespace is pure typed enums, structs, newtypes.
survivors.reason: Merges the lowering-pipeline arrow: assembled-schema-before-sugar (av1q), re-emit toward AssembledSchema (cq2e), one-to-one tree before Rust (6cm6), fixed-point passes (5ydx).
Distinct arrow: core macros always-imported substrate vs lazy-loaded user macros. Added referents only.
Distinct: locked (Macro Input+ Output) declaration syntax with explicit Macro tag winning over contextual matching. Added referents only.

survivors.desc: Everything reading NOTA-shaped structure above the raw parser goes through typed structural macro nodes; surviving hand-parsing sites (e.g. the schema-next macro library) are violations to fix, and a shape structural macro nodes cannot express is surfaced to the psyche, not worked around. NOTA itself should have a schema, its generated Rust schema-derived rather than a hand-maintained parser island. Dispatch precedence among competing reference forms (built-in heads, then declared macros, then the generic catch-all) is reified as an explicit typed NOTA value, so every reader and the resolver consult one canonical declared precedence.
survivors.reason: Merges the no-hand-parsing / schema-derived-NOTA arrow: hand-parsing is a violation (v0n6), NOTA schema-derived not a parser island (z2nx), dispatch precedence reified as typed NOTA value (549v).

survivors.desc: The signal/sema macro derives the typed wire layer from one schema source: signal_channel! embeds the 64-bit Tier-1 micro header by default (LogVariant + frame_micro projection), standardizes the two-enum-namespace shape (ordinary + owner contracts sharing byte-0 namespace via golden-ratio split), derives the small object directly (no hand-impl for Tier 1), gives every enum a Help variant documenting its namespace (command docs hook into Help), emits NOTA encode/decode from the same source, and emits a VersionProjection between current and next schema versions as the upgrade path.
survivors.reason: Merges the signal/sema macro derivation cluster: header+two-namespace+Help (m91k), Help-variant docs (6ttq), Tier-1 64-bit header (ai4j), NOTA codec emission (l489), VersionProjection (p31j). One macro's derived outputs.
Distinct meta-guidance: push repetition back into the schema macro; more schema generation the better. Added referent only.
Distinct: NOTA formatter derivable from macro pattern definitions. Added referents only.

survivors.desc: At a struct field or enum variant position, an @-sigil with a type name and no preceding name derives the binding name from the type: @Topics ≡ topics@Topics (field = camelCase of type); @SomethingHoldingSomething ≡ a data variant named after the held type; @(Vec Topic) derives from the composite. Bare PascalCase at an enum position stays a unit variant; explicit name@Type still works. The EnumVariants macro also supports compact self-tagged variants where tag equals payload type: a one-element parenthesised form expands to tag-and-type; a parenthesised name with a brace body registers a named struct, with a bracket body a named enum. An inline enum-body list may declare names without marking unit vs data-carrying; the codec resolves against the type table (a type ⇒ data-carrying, else unit). Assembled model unchanged — Vec<(Name, TypeReference)> for fields, named Variant for enum variants; derivation at lowering.
survivors.reason: Merges the same-name / @-sigil shorthand cluster: @Type derivation (dqmc), self-tagged compact variants (52ro), shorthand motivation (nryo), inline-list resolution against type table (17l9).

survivors.desc: Macros are sugar in the schema layer: the brace-enum form {Variant Payload ...} expands to the canonical paren-list ((Variant Payload) ...) — both lower to the same AssembledSchema, where variants are already (VariantName payload-option) records in a homogeneous vector. The macro engine dispatches on multiple criteria — delimiter, internal shape, root-object count, symbol qualification, and combinations — choosing the most specific. Enum-body vectors honor homogeneity: each element is one variant declaration — a bare PascalCase symbol (unit) or a parenthesised (Name Type) record (data); the older interleaved at-suffix VariantName@ PayloadType is retired as dishonest. The macro shape itself is two-position: pattern it matches, then template for how it expands.
survivors.reason: Merges brace-enum-sugar / multi-criteria-dispatch / pattern-template (3uae) with the vector-homogeneity correction of enum-body source form (506w).

survivors.desc: The schema-derived stack (nota-next, schema-next, schema-rust-next, signal-frame.schema, spirit.schema) does not reference Nexus, a separate NOTA-using vocabulary track (nexus + nexus-cli). Reserved sigils ~@!?*= are reserved at the NOTA syntax layer but the schema stack does not consume them; schema macros are plain NOTA records dispatched by position+shape in the schema-next MacroRegistry, not sigils.
survivors.reason: Distinct boundary fact: Nexus separate from schema stack; schema macros are records not sigils. De-narrated.
Distinct: micro-macros as composable named building blocks. Added referents only.

survivors.desc: Macro dispatch is two-phase: a structure-match macro first asks what shape the NotaValue is (enum short-syntax? struct? newtype?), then the matching per-shape transformation macro applies its lowering — they compose because the same syntactic placement can carry different shapes. Schema variants are ordinary vectors or macros, and each macro carries its own schema-reading logic, customizing interpretation by structural shape (e.g. curly-bracket with a single identifier; curly-bracket with even item count where all key positions resolve to a consistent type) rather than positional tokens. This is the schema language's extension mechanism; new shapes arrive as new macros enter the precompiled core or per-schema imports.
survivors.reason: Merges two-phase structure-match/transform dispatch (pul9) with macros-bring-own-reading-logic-by-shape (kkq3, u0ik are duplicates of each other).

survivors.desc: The schema lowering engine producing AssembledSchema is built from reusable data-carrying macro-variant lowerers at node-definition points: each macro-schema variant carries a struct defining its input type, and at a node-definition the builtin engine dispatches by shape to the matching variant, which consumes its input struct to produce lowered output. The engine is extensible by adding new variants plus their input structs, not by hard-coding shape rules. SchemaMacro in authored macro-library source is a typed/tagged variant carrying the macro-definition payload (not an untyped sentinel string), collected by the macro-library container before projection to serialized macro-library data.
survivors.reason: Merges extensible-lowering-engine-via-data-variants: sd7x (full), xayx (restatement), ox2x (builtin variants data-carrying at node points), jiy2 (SchemaMacro a typed variant carrying payload).

survivors.desc: The bootstrap core schema (the schema-schema) is implemented in ordinary Rust first and exposed as the macro interface for writing schema macros: it names macro inputs by type (a macro's input type IS its name in the macro), so macros inspect Nota block structure and emit assembled schema data, and people build their own macros against this core. Every schema file is loaded with the schema-schema attached implicitly; it defines the structural shape expected for schema files (root struct + sections) and is the lowest-level macro primitive everything builds on. Built-in macros bootstrap expansion before the user-declared macro space (the schema's creator space for typed macros) expands.
survivors.reason: Merges the schema-schema/bootstrap-core-interface cluster: sanf (core in code, names inputs by type), uti9 (schema-schema is the macro interface), w9gq (implicit schema-schema, lowest primitive), t0e1 (built-ins bootstrap before user space), bsnt (creator space).

survivors.desc: Macro loading uses a first indexing pass that collects all macro names from the schema and its imports before any macro is invoked; later passes resolve references by name from the index, enabling forward and out-of-order references across imports, with lazy loading (a macro loads only when referenced during dispatch). Expansion needs indexed reading order with lazy resolution, and passes may hand generic NOTA subobjects to later passes. Macro handlers receive NotaBody streams with outer wrapper delimiters stripped at match time, not the wrapper.
survivors.reason: Merges the macro-loading/resolution-mechanics cluster: indexing-pass with lazy forward refs (ydpa), indexed reading order + lazy resolution (jjhi), passes hand subobjects forward (rxzr), handlers get delimiter-stripped body (y1n5).

survivors.desc: Schema macro resolution maps nodes onto a closed macro space whose variants are the built-in type forms (Enum, Struct, Vec, Option, KeyValue, Newtype) via dual dispatch: look at the head keyword first, and if it is a reserved built-in (Vec, Option, KeyValue) dispatch directly and skip structural mapping; otherwise the node is a user type declaration resolved by structural shape — parenthesis holding an inner parenthesis is an enum, a bracket is a struct or newtype. In a strict brace key-value context a key-value pair is one semantic macro-node preserving the NOTA brace: qualified key plus brace value matches the struct macro, plus bracket value the enum macro, plus atom/reference value the newtype macro. Adds a keyword fast-path over the structure-and-position MacroRegistry.
survivors.reason: Merges dual-dispatch keyword-fast-path (1fgo) with brace key-value-to-macro mapping (rfg9) — both map a node onto built-in macro variants by keyword/shape.

survivors.desc: Open macro-node design frontiers, represented as macro-node configuration/data rather than parser magic: macro bodies need an explicit binding-and-reference mechanism for assigned symbols (a sigil such as dollar-sign is a candidate, the firm constraint being references stay visible in the schema language); and single-object sigil sugar where a leading sigil makes one NOTA object stand for a larger pair or field expression when the sigil makes the intended macro case obvious.
survivors.reason: Merges the two exploratory sigil/binding frontier records (e8iu binding mechanism, uxiz single-object sigil sugar) — both open, data-not-magic frontiers.

survivors.desc: The assembled schema is a live serializable artifact — NotaDecode, NotaEncode, and rkyv on Asschema and all substructure — with the in-memory typed Rust value as bootstrap only; the Rust emitter consumes the serialized assembled form (checked-in or build-time-emitted .asschema NOTA files), not the in-memory value directly.
survivors.reason: Both describe the serialized-schema pipeline: n9ta says the assembled schema must be serializable, 5pfy says the emitter consumes that serialized form. One arrow.

survivors.desc: Schema is self-hosting all-the-way-down: NOTA's own grammar and types are described by the foundational schema, which generates the Rust that interprets NOTA; the authoring language is itself a macro expanding to assembled-schema data, so the schema-of-schemas generates its own assembled and short-syntax forms. Schema is the load-bearing crystallization of earlier ASCII/component work — self-describing code, wire format, and docs from one .schema file.
survivors.reason: g2xr (NOTA schema-derived all-the-way-back), g9no (authoring language is a self-hosting macro loop), e7pe (schema crystallizes architecture, self-describing code+wire+docs) all express the schema engine's self-hosting/self-derivation principle.

survivors.desc: Schema files form namespaces (prefix initially the file name) mirroring Rust-style module decomposition: qualified colon-separated names or names split across module files, resolved via typed import variants (all, from path, from repo root, adjacent directory, git; namespace imports with optional selection) and re-exports, never flat-global. Entries can reference other entries, enabling recursive types and meta-schema self-bootstrap.
survivors.reason: 6jdv (namespace prefix from file name), akh5 (Rust-style module decomposition, qualified names, typed import variants), ernc (namespace imports with optional selection), 78cn (recursive type references / meta-schema bootstrap) all describe one schema namespace/module/import system.
Distinct still-relevant arrow: CLI-with-NOTA forces the daemon protocol to be binary. Aligns with the daemon-binary-startup override.
Unique engine-design arrow: tree-vs-tree (enum-against-enum) matching, cross-product as an explicit relationship node via a trait rather than ad-hoc match arms.
Small but distinct arrow: schema macros compose from reusable micro-macros.

survivors.desc: The schema type — which of the three planes (Sema, Nexus, Signal/Data) a schema declares — is a Kind enum schema::Kind, each variant carrying that plane's schema body (the four-position content), so a schema document's root is a Kind value. This gives the three typed engines their dispatch point and mirrors the Sema runtime envelope at the message level. Kind-versus-Plane naming still open.
survivors.reason: Distinct still-open design arrow on how the schema type is declared (variant-at-root Kind enum). Condensed from narration referencing prior open records; naming-open caveat preserved.

removed.reason: Settled implementation detail now living in code: the emit_schema! macro name vs signal_schema!/signal_channel!, with legacy fencing during a migration window since concluded. Supersedes-bookkeeping of operator/184; no durable still-relevant arrow beyond the self-hosting/emission survivors.

survivors.desc: A type's kind is announced at its declaration by the delimiter wrapping its value: brace body = struct, square-bracket body = enum, never inferred from syntactic-slot position. Generics use the reserved pipe-parenthesis delimiter, Name (| [params] body |) — params the binder list, body a nested struct/enum. nota-next already parses PipeParenthesis/PipeBrace, so this is schema-level work; use sites resolve by name like Vector/Map/Optional.
survivors.reason: Both express one arrow: type kind explicitly marked at declaration via wrapping delimiter, generics on the pipe-parenthesis form. hh3z refines 3742's principle into the concrete delimiter mechanism.

Distinct arrow: multi-argument type references use NOTA's flat positional head-then-arguments form, not grouped delimiters. Settles the Map reference shape. Not covered by the kind-declaration records.

survivors.desc: In schema, camelCase names are fields (instances); PascalCase declares or references a TYPE. Field names DERIVE by lowercasing the type name — an entry is just a list of type names (Entry (Topic Kind Summary ...)). A PascalCase type may be declared inline at first use, reused by name; resolution is declare-before-use, single-pass. Top-level namespace entry makes a type export-visible. Divergent field names use a newtype (Certainty (Magnitude)); the bare name-type form declares a distinct newtype, aliases lose it.
survivors.reason: All six circle one arrow: PascalCase-type / camelCase-field convention, field names by lowercasing, newtypes for divergent names (4fwy reverses the SchemaField addition, qz6j the bare newtype form). 7oqk and mchq are clauses of the same rule.

survivors.desc: The schema type system reduces to one canonical kind plus the unit case: everything is a STRUCT (data-carrying) or a UNIT VARIANT. Preferred shape is enum or vector-of-enum-variants; a Vector is structurally an enum (choice of all its members), a Vec a single-field struct. Maps are reserved for namespaces. Enum data-carrying variants ARE structs; variants are unit (bare PascalCase) or data-carrying (parenthesized), positional, in order. Bool's True/False is a two-unit-variant enum — why NOTA capitalizes booleans.
survivors.reason: All three are the structural-reduction arrow: struct + unit-variant as canonical kinds, enum/vector preference, PascalCase booleans as unit variants (5386 restates lmzl's boolean clause).

survivors.desc: The NOTA schema namespace section is a key-value MAP {key value ...} of user-defined types — keys are type names, values are definitions — not a flat sequence of separate declarations. Inline enum: EnumName (Variant1 Variant2 ...), parens holding variant names, variants themselves possibly nested enums. Structs are a different syntactic shape. Universal Unknown injection stays a behind-the-scenes macro mechanism; no user-authored Features section.
survivors.reason: Distinct arrow: namespace section is a map of named types, not a flat sequence; plus inline enum shape and Unknown-injection-as-macro. Not subsumed by the naming or reduction merges.

survivors.desc: Pipe-brace traits and impls are a data catalog referencing impls/methods already on the Rust side, so schema can name them and a method-calling syntax invokes them from schema bodies. An impl fuses onto its target type: a type declaration carries a trailing pipe-brace impl block, target implicit. Each entry is a bare trait atom (marker), a trait atom plus bracketed method signatures (body-bearing impl), or a bare signature (inherent method); a signature is name + brace parameter struct + return type, naming an existing impl. Calls resolve from receiver shape plus this catalog.
survivors.reason: Unique arrow: pipe-brace impls/traits as a fused data catalog over existing Rust impls, with the method-signature shape. No other record covers impl syntax.

survivors.desc: The entire built-in type-name vocabulary lives in Schema, not NOTA: scalars String, Integer, Boolean, Path and composites Vec, Optional, Map, Set, Box are Schema's type-reference vocabulary, alongside struct/enum declarations. NOTA stays pure structure (its delimiters) plus the codec and value literals None/(Some x). The (Vec X) tagged FORM and value-vs-type distinction hold ([X] is a vector value, (Vec X) the type) but live at the Schema layer; the codec stays in nota-next as serialization. Refines record 1137.
survivors.reason: Distinct layering arrow: type-name vocabulary belongs to Schema, NOTA stays pure structure + codec. Refines record 1137. Not redundant with other survivors.

Unique rationale arrow: NOTA as a typed-text-UI hack where every well-formed expression decodes reliably to known types, including typed macro extensions. The deeper rationale for NOTA-everywhere. Kept verbatim.
Distinct syntax arrow: at a slot whose enum type is already known, the value may omit the outer enum type name and supply the body directly, variant tags remaining named. Not covered elsewhere.

Distinct arrow: deep (6-7 layer) nesting is the normal schema shape; cross-references resolve through a flat type index to one canonical declaration; the engine traverses via canonical recursive walks, no per-layer handling.

survivors.desc: The schema root plane surface is a data-carrying enum named Plane with Signal, Nexus, and Sema variants carrying the actual plane messages, so runtime code matches directly on the plane rather than pairing a thin kind tag with a separate envelope. This supersedes the earlier thin per-plane envelope-type approach while keeping its goal: engine trait signatures type-check on plane membership (a signal-plane message cannot pass where a nexus/sema message is expected), so the trait chain is impossible to mis-wire on order and plane axes.
survivors.reason: Same arrow: type-enforced plane membership. ugig's data-carrying Plane enum supersedes qvod's thin-envelope mechanism but inherits its type-checking-on-plane intent; merged with ugig's wording as the live form.

removed.reason: Settled implementation detail of SignalCore: Sub-ID as a content-address primitive for non-Criome-identity entities, stored in ARCA. A code/architecture concern living in the implementation, no longer a load-bearing durable type-system arrow.

survivors.desc: Tests prove real behavior: every pipeline step is genuinely implemented end-to-end and does its real job; green tests alone are not correctness. Audit tests for genuineness, prefer real-binary-via-Nix proof over in-process synthetic state, and when blocked build the minimal real version of the missing piece IN the test. Forbid stubs-dressed-as-done, circular golden tests re-reading the engine's own output, per-file readers standing in for the shared codec, and text-round-trip black-boxes.
survivors.reason: All four assert the core anti-pretend-test arrow: genuine end-to-end implementation, unblock-in-the-test, real-binary over synthetic, forbidden stand-ins. Merged into one dense affirmative rule.

survivors.desc: Every load-bearing intent statement manifests as a constraint test (falsifiable spec or type-system witness per skills/architectural-truth-tests.md) that fails if the intent is not honored — structural/behavioral pins, not principles-in-docs. Audits feed bead filing toward constraint-tightening and end-to-end sandboxed daemon-to-daemon integration coverage.
survivors.reason: Both express bringing intents into constraint tests; hnoo adds the audit-to-bead-filing channel feeding constraint tests plus integration coverage. Same arrow.

survivors.desc: Proof of build/deploy/test claims must compile, execute, or round-trip the real path; grep is allowed only as a narrow negative guard proving a retired symbol or forbidden pattern is absent, never as positive proof. Test-claims cite a concrete file path, the invocation, and the witness output; the workspace audits whether claims match reality. A quiet ~27-minute local check is suspect unless it is a known heavyweight build (e.g. Rust toolchain).
survivors.reason: All three concern verifiable proof of claims vs false signals: no positive grep, claims must cite witness, suspect quiet runs. Merged.

survivors.desc: Tests across the nota/schema/sema stack load real separate .nota/.schema/.sema files via a small shared read-fixture helper, never inline source strings. Every .schema fixture is NOTA-legal, parsable by nota-next before lowering, and uses local relative imports. Real file-based tests prove each step: structural parse, the shared nota-next codec, schema-of-schemas read, macro assembly into macro-free Asschema data, the roots model, Rust datatype/derive emission over the shared codec, and the actor system running full constraints on spirit-next.
survivors.reason: All four: tests drive real .schema/.nota/.sema files (not inline strings), NOTA-legal fixtures, local imports, file-based proof of each pipeline step. One arrow about real-file fixtures across the stack.

survivors.desc: Integration tests exercise the actual execution chain through trait surfaces with per-plane schema-typed values throughout: real .schema files drive Nix codegen; the Nix-built CLI and daemon launch; the CLI sends real rkyv signal frames over a real Unix socket; signal-engine (Signal->Signal), nexus-engine (Nexus->Nexus plus translations), and sema-engine (SEMA->SEMA durable state) process and reply end-to-end. Never bypass the trait surface or collapse typed events to tokens; assert Signal/Nexus/SEMA boundaries were entered through engine methods at runtime, on typed schema-emitted outputs.
survivors.reason: All four describe the canonical typed-trait-surface integration test shape (k9kl full chain, z2cr per-plane typed values, js3u no-bypass, 4wdp runtime-entry trace). Same canonical shape.

survivors.desc: Real-world end-to-end testing uses the most real production topology; sandbox shortcuts that omit a load-bearing piece are not e2e. For the message-passing engine, true e2e is a real agent sending a real message to another real agent whose response returns to the first; a routed daemon path ending at a fake terminal or acceptance socket is only a routing-harness witness, not full e2e.
survivors.reason: Both define what genuine e2e/real-world testing requires: full topology, real-to-real round trip, not a fake terminus. Merged.

survivors.desc: QEMU/nixosTest VM checks run only on a node carrying the typed VM-testing service and VmHost KVM-hosting capability; the runner is selected by that service capability, never by whichever machine an agent runs on. Agents verify the VM-testing host boundary before invoking qemu-backed checks or ask for the correct host; ordinary nodes remain compile/evaluation surfaces. Generalizes the heavy-work-placement rule to VM testing.
survivors.reason: Both state the VM-testing host boundary: qemu/nixosTest only on the VmHost-capability node, selected by capability not current machine. Merged.

survivors.desc: The criome/lojix cluster is validated by a fully-networked multi-node test substrate with the spirit gate authenticated end-to-end through one reusable interface. One identical propagation test targets three substrates: a default hermetic host-untouched runNixOSTest VM cluster on prometheus; an opt-in durable on-demand microvm node on prometheus (BootOnce-activated, a typed horizon-rs NodeService, router/production untouched); and opt-in DigitalOcean droplets for cross-machine validation. The lojix production-cutover e2e deploys a full OS into a throwaway KVM VM (prometheus MAY carry the typed VmHost capability) and proves it survives SSH disconnect. The gate accepts an authorized head and rejects an unauthorized one fail-closed, building from 1-of-1 local toward multi-machine quorum.
survivors.reason: All four converge on the criome/lojix VM-cluster validation target: se72 cutover-into-VM, 7let host-untouched clarification, 77ic durable on-demand microvm tier, cpip three-substrate spirit-gate propagation test. One evolving decision.

survivors.desc: CriomOS gains a node feature for VM-based testing using the best Linux VM tech: a real display/GPU/DRM-capable VM (headless wlroots lacks wlr-gamma-control), Nix-native, CI-automatable and interactively viewable, to test CriomOS and visual/UI/GPU components end-to-end — launch the real app, run the component, observe the actual visual effect (theme/warmth/brightness) while watching for freezes and errors. chroma is the first target; sandboxes can run via systemd on a node such as Prometheus.
survivors.reason: Both: a real display/GPU VM feature for testing CriomOS visual components (chroma warmth/brightness) end-to-end, observing actual visual effect not just compile. Merged.
